# Project Status

## Overall Progress

- **Total active syntactic sorry sites**: **14** (compile-verified
  iter-099 close, unchanged from iter-098 close). Per-file distribution
  (verified by direct grep + `lean_diagnostic_messages`
  returning `[]` on whole file at iter-099 close):
  - `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` ŌĆö **6** at
    **L811, L903, L1227, L1255, L1445, L1474**. Iter-099 close note:
    no sorry closed this iteration; line numbers shifted +43 from
    iter-098 close (L768ŌåÆL811, L860ŌåÆL903, L1184ŌåÆL1227, L1212ŌåÆL1255,
    L1402ŌåÆL1445, L1431ŌåÆL1474) due to inserted S1ŌĆōS3 partial-chain
    tactics + structural-comment block at L765ŌĆōL810 inside
    `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`. Iter-099 prover committed a structural
    advance: the iter-101 plan's S1ŌĆōS3 chain (`simp only [Pi.smul_apply]`,
    `show ŌĆ” (Pi.ŽĆ ZŌéé j').hom _`, 4-layer `ŌåÉ ConcreteCategory.comp_apply`)
    landed cleanly. Six iter-099 routes for S4 scalar `(-1)^Ōåæi ŌĆó`
    extraction (`rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]`, `simp_rw`-variant,
    `simp only [ŌåÉ ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, Preadditive.zsmul_comp]`,
    **body-local `have h : (n ŌĆó f).hom x = n ŌĆó f.hom x := by intros; rfl`
    then `simp only [h]` / `rw [h]`**, in-place `set Pi_lift_thing
    : (ŌłÅßČ£ ZŌéü) Ō¤Č _ := Pi.lift fun i_1 Ōå” ...`) all failed at the same
    discrimination-tree blocker through Pi.lift's anonymous-closure
    codomain. **Critical finding**: the body-local rfl-helper escape
    route (E1 in iter-100/iter-101 plans) is now CONFIRMED DEAD ŌĆö
    pattern-match runs before rfl-evaluation. Hard cap 6 met,
    target of 5 missed. **STREAK CRITERION TRIGGERED**: 3rd
    consecutive prover lane on this sorry without closure; refactor
    lane mandatory for iter-100.
  - `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` ŌĆö **5** at L122, L636
    (`cotangentExactSeq_structure case h_exact` ŌĆö Route A/B decision
    pending), L957, L974, L1116. Not touched iter-100.
  - `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` ŌĆö **1** at L173
    (`instIsMonoidal_W`; Mathlib upstream gap; off-limits since
    iter-081).
  - `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` ŌĆö **1** at L179
    (`nonempty_jacobianWitness`; Phase C/E packaging; iter-101+).
  - `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` ŌĆö **1** at L190
    (`PicardFunctor.representable`; gated on Phase C C0ŌĆōC3).
  - **Active prover targets (post iter-099)**:
    - **`BasicOpenCech.lean` L811** (`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` step (c)
      per-summand `hG` post-S3): **POST-S3 FRAME LANDED.**
      Iter-099 committed the iter-101 plan's S1ŌĆōS3 chain (
      `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]; show ŌĆ” (Pi.ŽĆ ZŌéé j').hom _;
      rw [..., ŌåÉ ConcreteCategory.comp_apply (├Ś4)]`). The post-S3
      goal is now:
      `(ConcreteCategory.hom (((-1)^Ōåæi ŌĆó Pi.lift_thing) Ōē½ eqToHom Ōŗ»
       Ōē½ Pi.ŽĆ ZŌéé j')) (eŌéü.symm (r' ŌĆó y')) =
       r' ŌĆó (ConcreteCategory.hom (((-1)^Ōåæi ŌĆó Pi.lift_thing) Ōē½
       eqToHom Ōŗ» Ōē½ Pi.ŽĆ ZŌéé j')) (eŌéü.symm y')`.
      S4 (scalar extraction) is the residual blocker ŌĆö six routes
      including **both** the body-local rfl-helper (E1 in plans) and
      the in-place `set Pi_lift_thing` (E2-in-place) FAIL at the
      same discrimination tree. **MANDATE for iter-100**: refactor
      lane introducing a top-level named-codomain helper
      `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul_summand_via_named` with morphism family
      `T : ŌłĆ j, ZŌéü (h j) Ō¤Č ZŌéé j` (precedent: iter-098 split-slot
      pattern). Tactic-only route is FORBIDDEN.
    - **`BasicOpenCech.lean` L1445** (`g_R.map_smul'` area):
      becomes ungated once L811 closes ŌĆö iter-101+ priority.
    - **`Differentials.lean` L636** (`cotangentExactSeq_structure
      case h_exact`): Route A/B decision pending. Deferred Lane 2.
  - **5 long-standing transient (deferred)**: `Differentials.lean`

    or `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1`).

## Iteration log

### Iter-099 ŌĆö alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp body CLOSED; L695 bridge applied; hG residual at L728 awaits iter-100

- **Iteration shape**: refactor lane (iter-098 slug
  `alt-sum-pi-smul-aux-sum-comp`) + prover lane (iter-099 single
  substantive lane on `BasicOpenCech.lean`). Both lanes scheduled
  inside the same Archon iteration (harness `iteration: 97`).
- **Refactor lane outcome (iter-098)**: inserted
  `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` at L513ŌĆōL531 with `sorry`
  body at L532. The new top-level theorem splits the iter-097
  single-slot morphism family `F : ╬╣' ŌåÆ (ŌłÅßČ£ ZŌéü Ō¤Č ŌłÅßČ£ ZŌéé)` into
  `(G : ╬╣' ŌåÆ (ŌłÅßČ£ ZŌéü Ō¤Č ŌłÅßČ£ Z_int), E : ŌłÅßČ£ Z_int Ō¤Č ŌłÅßČ£ ZŌéé)`, with
  per-summand hypothesis on `G i Ōē½ E`. Universe hygiene
  `╬╣_int : Type u`. No protected signatures touched, no existing
  decls modified. Status `COMPLETE` per
  `.archon/logs/iter-097/refactor-alt-sum-pi-smul-aux-sum-comp-report.md`.
  Sorry count: 14 ŌåÆ 15 (one new structural sorry).
- **Prover lane outcome (iter-099)**:
  - **Sorries closed**: **1** (L532 `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`
    body); total 15 ŌåÆ 14.
  - **Step 1 (L532)**: SOLVED on first probe. Recipe
    `intro r y; rw [Preadditive.sum_comp s G E]; exact
    alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux ZŌéü ZŌéé s (fun i Ōå” G i Ōē½ E) eŌéü eŌéé
    hG r y` (3 tactic lines + 3 comment lines, L532ŌĆōL537). HOU-free
    because `G` and `E` are binders.
  - **Step 2 (L695 ŌåÆ now L728)**: **PARTIAL ŌĆö bridge applied; hG
    residual open.** The bridge
    `rw [ŌåÉ Pi.smul_apply (i := j)]; refine congrFun
    (alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp ZŌéü _ ZŌéé Finset.univ _ _
    eŌéü eŌéé ?_ r y) j` (L700ŌĆōL712) successfully applies the iter-098
    split-slot lemma via Miller-pattern unification: `Z_int`, `G`,
    `E := eqToHom Ōŗ»`, `s := Finset.univ` filled from the literal
    goal shape. **eqToHom lands in `E`'s independent slot**,
    bypassing the iter-097 Attempt-5 index-type dead-end. Residual
    `?_` is the per-summand `hG` R-linearity hypothesis at L728.
  - **hG discharge ŌĆö six routes failed**:
    - (2a) `simp only [Preadditive.smul_comp, ModuleCat.hom_smul, ŌĆ”]`
      ŌĆö `Preadditive.smul_comp` is **NOT** a Mathlib constant
      (correct names `Linear.smul_comp` / `Preadditive.zsmul_comp`).
    - (2b) `simp only [Linear.smul_comp]` ŌĆö pattern not found;
      polymorphic `(-1 : ?S)^(Ōåæi : ŌäĢ)` blocks unification.
    - (2c) `simp only [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]` / `[zsmul_comp]` ŌĆö
      same pattern blocker (Ōäż-route).
    - (2d) `rw [Linear.smul_comp]` ŌĆö same pattern blocker.
    - (2e) `simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply,
      ModuleCat.hom_smul, LinearMap.smul_apply, map_smul]; rfl` ŌĆö
      only `ModuleCat.hom_comp` + `LinearMap.comp_apply` fired;
      `ModuleCat.hom_smul` typeclass chain
      `[Monoid S][DistribMulAction S _][SMulCommClass k S _]` fails
      to synthesise with polymorphic `?S`.
    - (2f) `simp only [neg_smul, one_smul, smul_neg, pow_succ,
      pow_zero]` ŌĆö no progress.
  - **Committed partial** at L726ŌĆōL728:
    `intro i _ r' y'; simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp,
    LinearMap.comp_apply]; sorry` so iter-100 starts from a
    partially-distributed goal.
- **Substantive structural advance**: **YES.** Two distinct advances:
  1. **L532 body closed** via 3-line tactic chain ŌĆö the iter-098
     refactor's design hypothesis vindicated (`G`, `E` binders make
     `Preadditive.sum_comp` HOU-free).
  2. **L695 bridge applied** ŌĆö the iter-098 lemma fires HOU-free at
     the call site via Miller-pattern unification, finally
     bypassing iter-097's Attempt-5 index-type dead-end. The
     remaining `hG` sub-goal is qualitatively easier than the L695
     sorry it replaces (per-summand R-linearity on one concrete
     summand, not a structural-bridge search).
- **Compile-verified**: `lean_diagnostic_messages` returns `[]` for
  severity=error on whole file at iter-099 close. Sixth consecutive
  compile-verified prover iteration (iter-092 + iter-093 + iter-094
  + iter-095 + iter-097 + iter-099; iter-096 + iter-098 were
  refactor lanes that left the file compiling too).
- **Prover tool usage**: 3 `Edit`s + 1 `Write` (task_result) /
  7 `lean_multi_attempt` / 5 `lean_diagnostic_messages` / 3
  `lean_goal` / 12 lemma searches (8 `local_search`/`loogle` + 4
  leansearch/hammer) / 1 `lean_run_code` / 4 Read / 5 Bash / 4
  TodoWrite / 1 `lean_hover_info` / 1 `lean_term_goal` / 3
  ToolSearch / 4 Grep. 63 events, 1202 s wall.
- **Iter-100 plan (recommended)**: **PROVER LANE ŌĆö close L728 hG
  residual.** Force-elaborate polymorphic `(-1)^Ōåæi` via
  `set s : Ōäż := (-1 : Ōäż)^(Ōåæi : ŌäĢ) with hs` (or `change` with
  explicit `: Ōäż` ascription), then `Preadditive.zsmul_comp` fires.
  Estimated 20ŌĆō40 tactic lines. See
  `session_97/recommendations.md` ┬¦ Priority 1 for the directive
  draft.
- **`attempts_raw.jsonl` is FRESH iter-099** (timestamps
  11:58:49ZŌĆō12:18:28Z fall inside the iter-097 prover stage window
  per `meta.json`). Third consecutive iteration without pre-processor
  staleness recurrence.
- **Streak status**: closed 1 sorry; second consecutive iteration
  with substantive structural advance (iter-097 closed Step 1 +
  iter-098 split-slot refactor + iter-099 Step 2 bridge applied).
  The refactor ŌåÆ prover production loop is producing one sorry
  closure per cycle.

- **Step 2 application failed** in five subsequent routes (suffices

### Iter-095 ŌĆö Three plan routes all blocked; refactor escalation required

- 0 sorries closed; only commit (cosmetic) was 4-line

  same eqToHom-bridged Pi-product type mismatch.
- **Compile-verified**: yes.

- **Iter-094**: eqToHom-blocker BREAKTHROUGH via `ŌåÉ ModuleCat.hom_comp` (L570); keyŌéé block (L580ŌĆōL588) proved; 0 sorries closed; compile-verified.
- **Iter-093**: step (bŌåÆc) bridge progress; keyŌéü block (L570ŌĆōL577) proved; HOU obstruction shifted from (b) to (bŌåÆc) ŌłśŌéŚ-unfolding; 0 sorries; compile-verified.
- **Iter-092**: foundation repairs (doc-comment/set_option, intro/letI reconstruction) + step (a) hom_sum_dist via ModuleCat.hom_sum; 0 sorries; LSP fixed via elan/bin symlink.

The E1 escape route from iter-100/iter-101 plans is CONFIRMED DEAD. **Plan-directive lesson**: `piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply` does not fire via `rw` in the post-funext frame where LHS uses `eŌéé x j'` (LinearEquiv coercion) ŌĆö use `show` def-eq pivot instead. Not assigned iter-100. iter-079 `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf` + iter-081ŌĆō084 work preserved byte-for-byte. |

## Knowledge Base

### RiemannRoch subproject ŌĆö known blockers (iter-001 review, 2026-06-20)

- **Degree-1 H┬╣ headline is NOT axiom-clean ŌĆö it transitively carries `sorryAx` via `injective_flasque`** *(iter-001, first-hand `lean_verify` confirmed; do-not-trust the "out-of-cone" claim).* The prover (H1Vanishing task result) and an in-file comment (`H1Vanishing.lean:781-783`) claim `H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero` does NOT depend on the open `IsFlasque.injective_flasque` (only the `n=0` base case). **Formally false:** the headline `:= HModule_flasque_eq_zero (ŌĆ”) 1`, whose induction's `succ` case calls `injective_flasque` (a typed sorry, blocked on Mathlib's missing `j_!` extension-by-zero). `lean_verify` on the headline ŌåÆ `{propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. A sorry-free `i=1`-only path is mathematically available but requires **factoring the `n=0` base case out of the induction** (structural refactor) ŌĆö that, not closing `injective_flasque`, is the cheapest unblock for headline #1. `% NOTE:` added to both blueprint proof blocks (`thm:H1_vanishing_flasque`, `lem:H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero_main`).
- **`rationalMap_order_finite_support` is FALSE as typed under `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`** *(iter-001, lean-vs-blueprint-checker + in-code counterexample L807-812; do-not-retry under current signature).* The `.Finite` conclusion fails on a non-quasi-compact integral locally-Noetherian scheme (affine line with infinitely many origins). The `sorry` at L831 fills a false proposition. Blueprint *statement* already carries the correct hyp (Noetherian) and even says the Lean signature should take `[IsNoetherian X]`; the **Lean signature drifted to `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`-only**. Fix = strengthen `principal`'s signature to `[IsNoetherian X]`/`[CompactSpace X]` (derive `CompactSpace C.left` from `IsProper C.hom`) project-wide in one coordinated pass ŌĆö it ripples to `principal`, `principal_apply/_hom`, `LinearEquivalence`, `principal_degree_zero`, `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`, and cross-file callers (`OCofP.lean`, `RationalCurveIso.lean`). Cross-file/protected-shape decision ŌĆö NOT a prover-lane task.
- **`eulerCharacteristic_shortExact_add` is FALSE without a coherence/finiteness hypothesis** *(iter-001, lean-vs-blueprint-checker, explicit counterexample; do-not-retry the 6 finiteness sorries in place).* Stated for an arbitrary `ShortComplex` of `ModuleCat kbar`-sheaves with only `S.ShortExact`; under Mathlib's `finrank = 0` convention Žć-additivity fails for non-coherent sheaves. 6 of the lemma's 7 sorries are `FiniteDimensional (HModule ŌĆ” XßĄó n)` ŌĆö **unprovable as typed** (signal of the wrong signature, NOT routine debt); only the 7th (`Subsingleton (Ext L S.XŌéü 2)`, Grothendieck degree-2 vanishing) is honest missing-Mathlib infra. Lemma not frozen; fix = add coherence/finiteness hyp, discharge at the concrete call site `eulerCharacteristic_of_shortExact_skyscraper`. Blueprint `lem:euler_char_shortExact_add` ALSO omits the coherence hyp that its own `def:eulerChar_curve` insists on ŌĆö internal inconsistency, fix both sides.
- **`sheafOf` `DŌēĀ0` general construction is done sorry-free except the smoothŌ¤╣DVR instance** *(iter-001, OcOfD; honest, lean-vs-blueprint-checker 0 must-fix).* The ~430-line subsheaf-of-`K_C` carrier/presheaf/sheaf-property construction is sorry-free; the ONLY gap is the localized `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left := Ō¤©fun _ => sorryŌ¤®` (every codim-1 stalk of a smooth dim-1 curve over `k╠ä` is a DVR). This is the project's deliberately-axiomatised hypothesis; closing it as a global instance from `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]` (a `mathlib-build` lane) makes the localized sorry vanish. Related: `sheafOf_singlePoint` needs OCofP's `private carrier*` de-privatised OR restatement as iso `Ōēģ` (strict `Sheaf` equality unreachable); `sheafOf_ses_single_add` needs coherent-sheaf tensor-exactness API absent from Mathlib.
- **`Scheme.Hom.ofFunctionFieldEmbedding` (function-field-determines-curve, Hartshorne I.6.12) has no Mathlib constructor** *(iter-001, WeilDivisor).* Blocks `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` and the residual of `principal_degree_zero` (both bottom out at Hartshorne II.6.9 deg-multiplicativity / ramification-inertia on the finite morphism `Žå : C ŌåÆ ŌäÖ┬╣`). `mathlib-build`-scale substrate (~several hundred LOC), outside a single prover lane.

Localisation transports freeness via `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule` + rank via `Module.finrank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free`; `FormallySmooth k T`/`EssFiniteType k T` assemble by composition (`FormallySmooth.of_isLocalization M` + `.comp`; `EssFiniteType.of_isLocalization`/`.comp`). **Two scheme-pin blockers remain** (each multi-piece, raw material PRESENT): **(B1)** chart-extraction ŌĆö from `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom` + `x`, produce affine `VŌłŗx`, the *non-global* `k╠ä`-algebra instance on stalk `T = ­ØÆ¬_{C,x}` (k╠ä not recoverable from the stalk type ŌåÆ thread via `letI`/binders), `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 k╠ä ╬ō(V)` via `IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension.mk` + `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk` + `Scheme.╬ōSpecIso`; **(B2)** residue-field rationality `╬║(x)=k╠ä` (discharges the two `Subsingleton` residue hyps) via Nullstellensatz ŌĆö **no single-call Mathlib lemma**, assemble finite-ext-of-alg-closed-is-trivial.

### Soundness Patterns

- **Typed-`sorry` on the *carrier of a definition* silently propagates `sorryAx` through every consumer's typeclass synthesis** *(NEW iter-193 Archon canonical ŌĆö lean-auditor iter193 finding, dominant project-wide soundness exposure.)* When a `noncomputable def Foo : Carrier := sorry` lands as a file-skeleton placeholder (e.g. `Pic0Scheme`, `PicScheme`, `QuotScheme`, `picSharp`, `divFunctor`, `abelMap`, `PicSharp`, `presheaf`, `PicSharp.etSheaf`), the soundness exposure is qualitatively WORSE than a `theorem foo := sorry`: every typeclass `[Bar (Foo C)]` that fires on `Foo` silently carries `sorryAx`. **Auditor recommendation**: guard the definition behind an existential `Nonempty (╬Ż' S : Over (Spec k), _)` (or similar) ŌĆö this keeps the *typeclass-synthesis* honest even when the body is sorry-bodied. The `╬Ż'`-bundle pattern is already used by other file-skeletons in the project (`jacobianWitness`, etc.). A `refactor` subagent (`pic-quot-relpic-carrier-soundness`) holding write_domain across the 4 affected files (`Picard/{IdentityComponent, FGAPicRepresentability, QuotScheme, RelPicFunctor}.lean`) can atomically re-shape all `:= sorry` definition carriers in one pass without changing downstream consumer signatures (since consumers can be re-shaped to `Classical.choice (nonempty_*)` extractions). Reusable pattern: ANY `noncomputable def F := sorry` on a load-bearing definition deserves the existential-bundle pattern, NOT the bare `:= sorry`.

- **Opening an `instance` body's sorry via `refine Ō¤©...Ō¤®; sorry` *expands* downstream sorryAx propagation rather than contains it** *(NEW iter-195 Archon canonical ŌĆö lean-auditor iter195 must-fix on `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean:746-772`.)* Iter-195 Lane I advanced the body of `instance instIsRegularInCodimOneProjectiveLineBar` from a bare `:= sorry` to `:= by refine Ō¤©fun Y => ?_Ō¤®; sorry` (exposing the per-prime-divisor obligation) AND simultaneously advanced the consumer `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` body's tactic chain (Steps A+B+C). The latter's binder list `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]` means the consumer now silently picks up `sorryAx` through more lines of in-scope tactic state than before ŌĆö the iter is a NET INCREASE in silent propagation surface, not a decrease. **Rule**: when partial structural progress on an instance body is committed (the `refine Ō¤©...Ō¤®` step), simultaneously demote `instance` ŌåÆ `theorem` and thread `haveI` at consumer sites ŌĆö otherwise the silent-propagation cost grows alongside the structural progress. Reusable for any iter where Lane progress simultaneously advances a sorry-bodied instance AND its consumer's body. (Companion to iter-193 IdentityComponent.lean entry below; the iter-194 IC.lean fix recipe applies mechanically.)

- **`sorry`-derived typeclass instances silently propagate `sorryAx` into every downstream typeclass synthesis automatically** *(NEW iter-193 Archon canonical ŌĆö lean-auditor iter193 finding, `IdentityComponent.lean:500-507`.)* A `private instance identityComponent_geometricallyConnected` whose body invokes a sorry-bodied helper `geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section` (L414-420) is qualitatively worse than a sorry-bodied **theorem** because consumers CANNOT opt out ŌĆö instance synthesis fires automatically on every `[GeometricallyConnected (IdentityComponent G).hom]` query. **Auditor recommendation**: either (a) demote to a non-instance lemma until the helper closes; or (b) gate the instance behind a separate `[Hypothesis_GC]` typeclass that callers must explicitly supply. Reusable: NEVER mark `instance` on a typeclass derivation whose body transitively invokes `sorryAx` ŌĆö only `lemma` / `def`.

- **ANTI-PATTERN ŌĆö laundering a conditional theorem's content into a FALSE unconditional sorry (the "dead hypothesis" tell)** *(NEW iter-157 Archon canonical ŌĆö both review subagents `lean-auditor-iter157` + `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-av-rigidity-iter157` independently caught this on `rigidity_lemma`/`rigidity_core`/`rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`.)*
  When a proof of a **conditional** theorem `P ŌåÆ Q` (here: `f` collapses a slice `Ō¤╣` `f` factors through `snd`) does **not consume its hypothesis `P`** and instead delegates to a helper lemma stating `Q` **unconditionally**, that helper is almost certainly **false**, and the theorem's apparent "proven modulo one sorry" status is **illusory** ŌĆö the residual sorry is unsatisfiable. **Iter-157 instance**: `rigidity_lemma` (Mumford Rigidity Lemma) compiled and reduced to a single `sorry` (`rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`), but its proof never used the collapse hypothesis `_hf : f(X├Ś{yŌéĆ})={zŌéĆ}`; the delegate lemmas `rigidity_core` (`f = retract Ōē½ f` for arbitrary `f`) and `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open` (a non-empty open of agreement, no collapse hyp) are **false** ŌĆö counterexample `f = fst` on `X=Y=Z=ŌäÖ┬╣` satisfies every stated instance (`IsProper`/`GeometricallyIrreducible`/`IsReduced`/`IsSeparated`) yet `fst ŌēĀ (x,y)Ōå”xŌéĆ`. **Detection recipe**: (1) spot a load-bearing theorem with a **dead hypothesis** (`_hf`/`yŌéĆ`/`zŌéĆ` unused by the proof ŌĆö `lean_goal` shows them inert; the proof term doesn't reference them); (2) inspect each delegate lemma it routes through for whether it OMITS that hypothesis; (3) **construct a counterexample within the delegate's stated instances** (the projection `fst`/identity `id` are the canonical refutations of "every map is coordinate-independent"). `lean_verify` showing `sorryAx` is NOT enough ŌĆö the axiom check confirms an *open* obligation but does not detect that the obligation is *false*. **Fix**: thread the dropped hypothesis back into the delegates (re-sign), then consume it in the wrapper proof. Reusable for any conditional-theorem formalization where a "geometric heart" is isolated into a helper: verify the isolated helper is still TRUE (carries the conditional data), not just that it type-checks. **RESOLVED iter-158 (both review subagents `lean-auditor-iter158` + `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-av-rigidity-iter158` independently re-verified):** the fix was executed exactly as prescribed ŌĆö `_hf` re-signed into `rigidity_core` + `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open` and threaded through `rigidity_lemma`, then GENUINELY CONSUMED in `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`'s body (`hcomp := congrArg Over.Hom.left _hf` Ō¤╣ `hyŌéĆ : yŌéĆpt Ōłē Gset` Ō¤╣ the non-emptiness witness `s xŌéĆpt`). The `f = fst` counterexample no longer satisfies the antecedent (`_hf` forces `­Ø¤Ö X = toUnit X Ōē½ zŌéĆ`, i.e. `X` collapses to a point). `rigidity_lemma`/`rigidity_core` are now sorry-free in their own bodies; the residual is two TRUE-as-stated sorries inside `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`. **The repair-validation recipe**: after threading, (1) confirm the dropped hyp is referenced by the proof term (not inert), (2) re-run the counterexample against the now-stronger antecedent to confirm it's excluded, (3) confirm `lean_verify` shows only `sorryAx` with NO custom axiom.

  In `Over (Spec k)` the cartesian-monoidal `ŌŖŚ` IS the chosen pullback, so `CategoryTheory.Over.snd_left : (snd R S).left = Limits.pullback.snd R.hom S.hom` and `Over.tensorObj_left : (R ŌŖŚ S).left = pullback R.hom S.hom` are **exact rewrites** (also `Over.fst_left`, `Over.comp_left`). To transport a base-change-stable scheme property `P f` (here `UniversallyClosed`, Ō¤╣ `IsClosedMap` via `Scheme.Hom.isClosedMap`) from `X.hom` to `(snd X Y).left`: `rw [Over.snd_left]` then `<morphismProperty>.IsStableUnderBaseChange.of_isPullback (IsPullback.of_hasPullback X.hom Y.hom) hp`.   In a `CartesianMonoidalCategory` (here `Over (Spec k)`), lift-equalities like `snd X Y Ōē½ lift (toUnit Y Ōē½ xŌéĆ) (­Ø¤Ö Y) = lift (toUnit (XŌŖŚY) Ōē½ xŌéĆ) (snd X Y)` close by `ext1 <;> simp` ŌĆö no need to name `comp_lift`/`lift_snd` (they resolve through the opened `CartesianMonoidalCategory` namespace). **Gotcha**: `toUnit_unique` is an *equality term*, not a function ŌĆö `exact toUnit_unique _ _` after a manual `congr` over-applies it (`Function expected at toUnit_unique ?m ?m but this term has type ?m = ?m`). **Downstream-import-cycle gotcha** (also iter-157): `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` lives in `AlgebraicJacobian.Rigidity`, which imports `Jacobian`, which imports the new upstream `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean` ŌĆö importing it creates a CYCLE. - **`lean_verify` axiom-check catches `sorryAx`-laundering consumers (a clean-compiling theorem can silently depend on a `sorry`)**
  *(NEW iter-151 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by `lean-auditor-chartalgebra-iter151` + `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-chartalgebra-iter151` on `GrpObj.df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`.)*
  A theorem whose body is a one-line delegate to a `sorry`-bearing lemma compiles with **NO `declaration uses 'sorry'` warning of its own**, yet `lean_verify`/`#print axioms` reports `sorryAx` in its axiom set. So a per-file `sorry` count (from `lean_diagnostic_messages` warnings or `sorry_analyzer`) **understates** the unsound surface: every clean-looking downstream consumer of a `sorry`-lemma is also unsound but invisible to the warning-based count. **Recipe**: when a lemma carries a `sorry` (especially a *known-false* one), run `lean_verify <FullyQualified.consumerName>` on each downstream consumer (find them via `grep -n` on the lemma name) to see which ones launder `sorryAx`. **Iter-151 instance**: `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` (`ChartAlgebra.lean:450`) delegates to the false KDM lemma and presents as a closed theorem while depending on `sorryAx`; the project's "9 sorries" headline therefore undercounts the actual unsound dependency set. Reusable whenever a `sorry` lemma has compiled consumers. **Iter-152 update (verified, both review subagents)**: after the alg-closed pivot, `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` (now `ChartAlgebra.lean:411`) STILL carries `sorryAx` transitively (verified via `lean_verify` ŌĆö axiom set `{propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`), but the laundering is now *honest*: the delegated KDM lemma is mathematically TRUE with a genuine open `sorry`, not a false-statement sorry. So the laundering-risk persists but its character changed from "unsound (false lemma)" to "incomplete (open obligation)". **The blueprint `\leanok` consequence is live**: `sync_leanok` marks df_zero's PROOF block `\leanok` (its own body is sorry-free) even though it transitively depends on the open KDM sorry ŌĆö a `% NOTE (iter-152 review)` was added to `RigidityKbar.tex` (df_zero statement block) to record this so the proof-block `\leanok` is not read as "fully discharged". Same pattern also holds for `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean:249`, transitive on the two witness sorries).

- **Counterexample-driven impossibility detection: when a "kernel-equals-constants" goal won't close, test it on disconnected and non-geometrically-connected algebras before grinding the proof**
  *(NEW iter-151 Archon canonical ŌĆö KDM `mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero` (C.d) transfer step.)*
  A goal of the shape "`D b = 0 Ō¤╣ b Ōłł range(algebraMap k B)`" (kernel of a derivation = constants) is FALSE for any `B` where `k` is not algebraically closed in `B`, even with `[CharZero k]` + smoothness/standard-smoothness. The two minimal counterexamples that satisfy `[Field k][CharZero k][FiniteType k B][IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B]` yet break the conclusion: (CE1) `B = k├Śk`, `n=0` (finite ├®tale Ō¤╣ `╬®=0` Ō¤╣ `D=0`; range = diagonal ŌŖŖ B) ŌĆö kills "add connectedness"; (CE2) `k=ŌäÜ`, `B=ŌäÜ(ŌłÜ2)`, `n=0` (finite separable Ō¤╣ ├®tale Ō¤╣ `D=0`; range = ŌäÜ ŌŖŖ ŌäÜ(ŌłÜ2)) ŌĆö kills "add `[IsDomain B]`". `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B` is *exactly* "Ōłā submersive presentation of dim n" (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Smooth/StandardSmooth.lean` L88) and carries **no** connectedness/geometric-connectedness data. The true hypothesis needed is "`k` algebraically closed in `B`" (ŌēĪ `B` geometrically integral / `Spec B` geometrically connected over `k`). **Recipe**: for any `ker(derivation) = base` claim on an abstract algebra, before attempting closure, instantiate `B = k├Śk` and a non-trivial separable field extension; if either violates the conclusion, the lemma needs a geometric hypothesis and the right move is an architectural signature change, NOT another helper. Reusable for every derivation-kernel / constants-extraction lemma. **Iter-152 resolution**: the architectural signature change this entry recommends was executed ŌĆö JOINT `[IsAlgClosed k]` + `[IsDomain B]` repairs KDM (see the "honest-wrong-signature" anti-pattern entry below for the full resolution). The two counterexamples are now excluded on both Lean and blueprint sides.

### Anti-Patterns (do not retry)

- **Anti-pattern: ship a `theorem foo : P := by sorry` where the in-source docstring openly admits `P` is mathematically false under stated hypotheses (the "honest-wrong-signature" pattern)**
  *(NEW iter-148 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by `lean-auditor-iter148` flagging `KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero` at `ChartAlgebra.lean:123` as the "weakened-wrong-definition" anti-pattern in its purest form.)*
  When a Lean declaration's body is `sorry` and the surrounding docstring openly admits the conclusion is FALSE under the stated hypotheses (iter-148 instance: `b Ōłł (algebraMap k B).range` fails for `B = k[X]`, `b = X^p` in characteristic `p`, with `[Field k] [CommRing B] [Algebra k B] [Algebra.FiniteType k B] (hDb : D b = 0)`), the declaration cannot be closed honestly without changing the signature. **Honesty about wrongness does not make the claim provable.** The pattern is morally equivalent to an excuse-comment (`-- TODO: replace`, `-- placeholder`) even when the docstring frames the wrongness as "the blueprint-mandated entry-point name" ŌĆö it documents *intentionally shipping a wrong signature with a permanent sorry*. **Rule**: if the docstring would need to admit the conclusion is FALSE under stated hypotheses, the signature must be inflated before the body can be filled ŌĆö OR the blueprint anchor name must migrate to a renamed lemma that is actually provable. The plan agent's "the consumer expects this name" framing is not sufficient ground to ship the wrong signature; the cost is a permanent invisible sorry that no future prover lane can discharge. **Iter-148 corrective path** for the KDM case: inflate to `[CharZero k] + [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension k B]` (and propagate to ╬▓-core consumer), or commit to (p1) char-p Cartier chain with `[Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k B]` + Frobenius hypothesis. Documented at `recommendations.md` (iter-148 review) ┬¦ "CRITICAL ŌĆö KDM signature". **RESOLVED iter-152**: the iter-149 `[CharZero k]`+std-smooth inflation was confirmed (iter-151) STILL false (counterexamples `B=k├Śk`, `ŌäÜ(ŌłÜ2)/ŌäÜ`); the architectural fix that actually works is the JOINT addition of `[IsAlgClosed k]` + `[IsDomain B]` to KDM (`[IsDomain B]` kills `B=k├Śk`; `[IsAlgClosed k]` kills `ŌäÜ(ŌłÜ2)/ŌäÜ`). Neither hypothesis suffices alone ŌĆö the iter-151 "one-at-a-time" reasoning was too pessimistic. Landed via the iter-152 `refactor isalgclosed-signatures`; KDM is now a genuine to-prove `sorry` (the field-of-fractions argument), no longer a false-statement sorry. The general rule stands: a `sorry` whose docstring admits the conclusion is false under stated hypotheses needs a signature change, not a helper round.

- **Anti-pattern: carry decorative typeclasses on a consumer that does not invoke its callee with them (the "consumer-with-unused-typeclasses" pattern)**
  *(NEW iter-148 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by `lean-auditor-iter148` + `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-chartalgebra-iter148` on `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` at `ChartAlgebra.lean:196`.)*
  When a consumer carries four `C`-side scheme typeclasses (`[Smooth (C Ōåś Spec k)]`, `[IsProper]`, `[IsReduced]`, `[GeometricallyIrreducible]`) and the body is a one-line wrapper around a helper that accepts none of them, the typeclasses are **decorative**. The signature *looks* like it has more chart-of-proper-curve context, but typeclass synthesis cannot bridge them through the helper call. Either (a) prune the unused typeclasses to align the signature with the actual body's commitment, or (b) inflate the helper's signature to consume them substantively. Maintaining the decorative typeclasses is the visual analog of the wrong-signature anti-pattern above ŌĆö the consumer reads as if it does more than its body actually does. **Rule**: every typeclass / hypothesis in a signature should be consumed somewhere in the body; if none of the body's term-level identifiers consume the typeclass, prune it or strengthen the body. **Iter-148 instance**: `ChartAlgebra.lean:196 GrpObj.df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` carries `[Smooth (C Ōåś Spec k)] [IsProper] [IsReduced] [GeometricallyIrreducible]` on `C` but the body is `KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero hDb` which consumes nothing from `C`. The iter-148 review-phase `% NOTE:` block on the blueprint side authorises the iter-148 thin-wrapper disposition pending iter-149+ substantive refinement; the Lean-side fix is queued as REC-3-bis. Reusable as a flag for any "consumer-of-named-helper" closure where the consumer's signature is wider than the helper's.

- **Signature-refinement-via-delegate: closing a `: True := sorry` placeholder by routing to a separately-named helper that itself carries the substantive sorry**
  *(NEW iter-147 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by the iter-147 prover lane on `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` ╬▓-core (`df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`).)*
  When the iter-N blueprint disposition concentrates the substantive content at a separately-named helper (the iter-146 B.preferred disposition for the chart-algebra (╬▓) chain: chart-of-proper-curve hypothesis pushed into `constants_in_chart_of_proper_curve`, leaving the consumer `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` as a one-line delegate), the iter-N+k prover can close the consumer as **sorry-free** by replacing its `: True := sorry` placeholder with the real signature and a body that delegates to the named helper. Requirements: (a) the consumer's hypotheses are exactly the standing premises the helper consumes internally; (b) the helper exists by name (even if its body is `sorry`); (c) namespace ordering lets the consumer reference the helper (iter-147 instance required re-ordering KDM to land **before** the second `namespace GrpObj` reopening so ╬▓-core could reference `KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero`). The consumer's body becomes a one-line `:= helper_name args`; net per-iter cost is ~10ŌĆō20 LOC. Reusable for any consumer-of-named-helper situation. Caveat: the helper's `sorry` is **not** discharged by this pattern ŌĆö the substantive content is just relocated to the helper. Track the helper sorry-count separately.

- **`rw [RingHom.range_eq_top]` exposes `Function.Surjective Ōćæf` as the next goal**
  *(NEW iter-147 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by the iter-147 prover lane on `constants_integral_over_base_field` substep (3).)*
  When the goal is `f.range = ŌŖż` for a ring hom `f` and the natural continuation constructs a surjectivity witness (rather than chasing the `Subalgebra` / `range` layer), `rw [RingHom.range_eq_top]` rewrites the goal to `Function.Surjective Ōćæf`. This composes cleanly with Mathlib bijectivity lemmas ŌĆö iter-147 instance: post-rewrite, the algclose-base-change chain consumes `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` (`Mathlib/FieldTheory/IsAlgClosed/Basic.lean`) to get the bijectivity of `algebraMap k ╬ō(X_{\bar k}, ŌŖż)`, which is the natural continuation. Without the rewrite, the chain has to fight the `Subalgebra`/`range` layer at every step (the rewrite eliminates the chain-internal `RingHom.range`/`AlgHom.range`/`Subalgebra` impedance mismatches). Reusable any time a `range = ŌŖż` claim has a natural surjectivity-style witness.

- **Multi-step closure chain pattern: in-source comments naming each substep's Mathlib lemma + one residual `sorry` concentrated at the substantive gap**
  *(NEW iter-147 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by the iter-147 prover lane on `constants_integral_over_base_field` substep (3) 7-step closure chain.)*
  When a closure chain has many substeps but only one is the substantive Mathlib gap (most others have catalogued Mathlib lemmas), commit the chain as in-source comments with each substep's target lemma named, place the residual `sorry` at the chain's exit (NOT at each substep), and document the named Mathlib gap immediately above the sorry. This preserves the iter-N+k continuation point without requiring the prover to chase every substep before committing. Iter-147 instance: substep (3) decomposed into 7 steps (a)ŌĆō(g) at `ChartAlgebra.lean:243ŌĆō293`; 6 steps name Mathlib lemmas (`IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral`, `Module.finrank_baseChange`, `Subalgebra.bot_eq_top_iff_finrank_eq_one`, `IsStableUnderBaseChange @GeometricallyIrreducible`, `isField_of_universallyClosed`, `finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`); step (e) (flat base change of `╬ō` for proper scheme; Stacks 02KH) is the residual gap, concentrated at the chain-exit `sorry`. The pattern reads honestly (no over-claim) and lets iter-148+ work the wrapper precisely at the named gap.

- **`Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra` is a `local instance` inside `Mathlib.RingTheory.IsTensorProduct`; downstream files must re-enable it at file scope to use the canonical `Algebra.IsPushout k BŌéü BŌéé (TensorProduct k BŌéü BŌéé)` instance**
  *(NEW iter-146 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by the iter-146 prover lane on `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` `algebra_isPushout_of_affine_product`.)*
  Mathlib's `TensorProduct.isPushout` instance (`Mathlib/RingTheory/IsTensorProduct.lean:630`) discharges `Algebra.IsPushout k BŌéü BŌéé (BŌéü ŌŖŚ[k] BŌéé)` directly ŌĆö but it requires both the left algebra instance on `TensorProduct k BŌéü BŌéé` (global) AND the right algebra instance (Mathlib ships as `local instance` inside `Mathlib.RingTheory.IsTensorProduct`). Downstream files trying to use `inferInstance` will fail with `failed to synthesize instance of type class Algebra BŌéé (TensorProduct k BŌéü BŌéé)`. The unlock is `attribute [local instance] Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra` at file scope. Documented in-file at `Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean:70ŌĆō74`. **Iter-146 instance**: the chart-algebra (╬▒) helper collapsed from a planned ~80ŌĆō150 LOC three-step Mathlib chain (`pullbackSpecIso` ŌåÆ `isPullback_SpecMap_of_isPushout` ŌåÆ `CommRingCat.isPushout_iff_isPushout`) to a 5-LOC `inferInstance` body once the unlock landed. The three-step chain is still the honest scheme-level identification; the algebra-level instance is what the chart-algebra consumers (╬▓-core + KDM + lift) actually need. **Caveat**: file-scope re-enablement widens the instance-search surface for every importer; if a narrower-scope option suffices (`local instance` inside a `section`), prefer that.

- **`isField_of_universallyClosed` + `finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed` chain for "╬ō(X, ŌŖż) is a finite-dim field extension of k" under `IsProper` + `[IsReduced X]` + `[GeometricallyIrreducible (X Ōåś Spec k)]`**
  *(NEW iter-146 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by the iter-146 prover lane on `constants_integral_over_base_field`.)*
  For `X : Scheme` smooth proper geometrically irreducible over a field `k`, the cleanest Mathlib chain to show `╬ō(X, ŌŖż)` is a finite-dim field extension of `k`:
  1. `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton (X Ōåś Spec k)` ŌåÆ `IrreducibleSpace X` (uses singleton-base `Spec k`).
  2. `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced X` ŌåÆ `IsIntegral X` (needs `[IsReduced X]` ŌĆö see Knowledge Base entry on `Smooth ŌćÆ IsReduced` gap).
  3. `[IsProper (X Ōåś Spec k)]` extends `UniversallyClosed` + `LocallyOfFiniteType` automatically.
  4. `isField_of_universallyClosed k (X Ōåś Spec k)` ŌåÆ `IsField ╬ō(X, ŌŖż)`.
  5. `finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k (X Ōåś Spec k)` ŌåÆ `appTop.hom` is finite.
  Combined: `╬ō(X, ŌŖż)` is a finite field extension of `k`. The geom-irr-forces-dim_k=1 step (substep 3) is then the iter-147+ closure path via `IsBaseChange` + `Module.finrank_baseChange`. Reusable for any chart-algebra-style argument that needs to reduce a `╬ō`-type claim to a dimension claim over the base field.

- **Named-concept-vs-body mismatch as iter-146 review-phase pattern (signature-reduction blueprint NOTE discipline)**
  *(NEW iter-146 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by the iter-146 prover lane on `Scheme.Over.ext_of_diff_zero` + paired auditor / lean-vs-blueprint-checker must-fix findings.)*
  When a prover refines a `: True := sorry` placeholder by delegating to a renamed-but-existing lemma (iter-146 instance: `ext_of_diff_zero` body is a one-line delegate to the iter-125 `ext_of_eqOnOpen` packaging, dropping the planner-spec'd `df = dg` hypothesis), the closure is sorry-free Lean but the blueprint statement and Lean signature drift out of sync. **Rule**: the blueprint MUST receive a `% NOTE: (iter-N review)` annotation flagging the deliberate signature reduction, naming what was dropped, and scheduling the iter-N+k re-refinement. Otherwise the next prover attempt at the load-bearing form will be confused about whether the lemma is already closed. **Honest-on-the-Lean-side alone is insufficient** ŌĆö both sides need to acknowledge the reduction. **Iter-146 instance**: `ChartAlgebra.lean:196ŌĆō203` docstring documents the reduction; `RigidityKbar.tex:lem:Scheme_Over_ext_of_diff_zero` received the `% NOTE: (iter-146 review)` annotation this review-phase pairing the call-out.

- **Mathlib `b80f227` lacks general `Smooth (X ŌåÆ Spec k) ŌćÆ IsReduced X` over a field; project carries `[IsReduced X]` explicitly at every call site**
  *(NEW iter-146 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by the iter-146 prover lane on `constants_integral_over_base_field`. Now documented at three sites: `Rigidity.lean`'s "Hypothesis history" (pre-existing), `Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean:140ŌĆō143` (iter-146 add), `RigidityKbar.tex:lem:constants_integral_over_base_field` `% NOTE: (iter-146 review)` (iter-146 review-phase add).)*
  Mathlib snapshot `b80f227` ships `Smooth ŌćÆ IsReduced` only for perfect-base via `Scheme.Hom.dense_smoothLocus_of_perfectField`. For a general base field `k`, no such direct lemma exists. The project's discipline is to explicitly carry `[IsReduced X]` at every call site that needs it. Once the Mathlib gap closes, the explicit-`IsReduced` hypothesis becomes derivable from `[Smooth (X ŌåÆ Spec k)]` and may be dropped at every site in tandem. **Do NOT** try to drop `[IsReduced X]` from a declaration relying on the chain "smooth ŌćÆ reduced ŌćÆ integral (under geom-irr)" until the Mathlib gap is closed. **Iter-146 instance**: `ChartAlgebra.lean:148` adds `[IsReduced X]` alongside `[Smooth (X Ōåś Spec (.of k))]`; the `IsIntegral X` instance synthesis at L156 chains through this explicit-`IsReduced` rather than through `Smooth`.

- **In-tree excise is the iter-141-discipline-consistent response to strategy-critic Q7 ("auditable record" of descoped route) findings**
  *(NEW iter-145 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by `strategy-critic-iter145` Q7 + `refactor-chart-algebra-skeleton-bundled-excise-iter145` execution.)*
  When a strategy-critic flags ~hundreds-of-LOC of sorry-bodied scaffolding as "preserved in-tree as auditable record" of a now-descoped route, the iter-141 anti-pattern (preservation-of-bundled-framing-as-silent-option) extends from prose to in-tree disposition. **Deletion is the disciplined response, not preservation.** Iter-145 instance: 3 named targets + 2 cascade-excised consumers totalling 272 LOC removed from `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean`; project-side audit record is preserved via (a) git history (`git log` / `git show`) for the deleted bodies, (b) `analogies/` design notes for design context (`analogies/chart-algebra-vs-bundled-iter144.md`, `analogies/d-app-d-map-recipe-shape.md`, etc.), (c) `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` sidecars for the per-iter trajectory. **Rule**: a refactor that ONLY changes disposition (not signatures) on sorry-bodied descoped declarations is the iter-141-discipline response. If the planner believes "auditable record" is the right disposition despite the Q7 challenge, it must write down a concrete falsification criterion ("We keep the scaffolding because [named future use]"); if no such use exists, deletion is correct.

- **Project-authorised `: True := sorry` placeholder + lean-auditor must-fix classification (subagent-semantics tension)**
  *(NEW iter-145 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by `lean-auditor-review145` flagging the 5 chart-algebra placeholders as must-fix despite the iter-145 refactor directive explicitly authorising them.)*
  When a plan-agent directive authorises `: True := sorry` placeholder signatures (per the iter-128 ŌåÆ iter-131 cautionary-tale "safer than committing wrong signatures" rule), the lean-auditor descriptor's "no strategic bias" stance mandates classifying them as must-fix (triple-hit: suspect-body + weakened-wrong-definition + excuse-comment). Both correct in their domain: the directive's authorisation is project-strategic; the auditor's classification is project-bias-blind by descriptor. **Future plan-agent directives that authorise `: True` placeholders should note the auditor's expected must-fix classification in `summary.md` / `recommendations.md` so review-phase findings are read in context.** The review-agent honors the project authorisation when triaging recommendations ŌĆö must-fix-this-iter from the auditor on a directive-authorised placeholder does NOT block downstream prover work if the placeholder is the iter-N+1 refinement target.

- **Pre-committed conditional-fallback chart-algebra pivot (mechanical-gate-honored rule)**
  *(NEW iter-144 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by the iter-144
  mandatory chart-algebra-vs-bundled re-evaluation gate firing with
  PIVOT_TO_CHART_ALGEBRA per
  `mathlib-analogist-chart-algebra-iter144` + persistent file
  `analogies/chart-algebra-vs-bundled-iter144.md`)*:
  When an iter-N `mathlib-analogist` surfaces a chart-algebra (or
  more generally: alternative-closure-path) alternative with a
  CONTINUE_BUNDLED criterion of the form "pivot unless ŌēźM
  sub-sorries closed iter-N+k" (or "pivot unless ŌēźM of K sub-sorries
  closed across iter-N+1..N+k"), and the bundled route does NOT
  mechanically meet the criterion in the budgeted K iters, the pivot
  must be honored even if an intervening iter-N+j strategy-critic
  has rejected a related-but-different "route-pivot" question. The
  CONTINUE_BUNDLED gate is the **authoritative trigger**;
  strategy-critic verdicts on adjacent route-pivot questions (e.g.,
  base-change) do NOT preclude honoring the mechanical gate on a

  chart-algebra analogist set CONTINUE_BUNDLED at "Ōēź2 sub-sorries
  closed iter-140"; iter-140 closed 0/3, cumulative iter-140/142/143
  closed 1/3 (d_map only); iter-141 strategy-critic rejected an

  (piece (i.b) Step 2 is base-independent) ŌĆö but the iter-144 pivot
  answers a closure-path question, not a base-change question. The
  mechanical gate fired cleanly; iter-144 PIVOT honored. **Rule**:
  pre-committed conditional fallbacks must be enforced at the
  scheduled gate, regardless of intervening strategy-critic
  framing ŌĆö the strategy-critic's territory is sunk-cost / soundness
