- **`PresheafOfModules`-morphism iso check via `isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R)` + `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`** *(NEW iter-140 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by the iter-140 prover lane structural refactor on
  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two`
  at `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:688`)*:
  The standard way to check that a morphism `f : M Ō¤Č N` of
  presheaves of modules over a presheaf of rings `R` is an
  isomorphism is to chain two Mathlib facts: (i)
  `PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R` is a `ReflectsIsomorphisms`-functor
  (needs `import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Functor.ReflectsIso.Balanced`
  for typeclass synthesis ŌĆö the file is `Balanced` + `Faithful` ŌåÆ
  `ReflectsIsomorphisms`); (ii) `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`
  reduces "iso of natural transformation" to "iso of each app".
  Combined, this reduces a `PresheafOfModules`-morphism iso check
  to a per-open `IsIso (f.app X)` check inside `ModuleCat (R.obj X)`.
  **5-LOC helper** (codified as
  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.isIso_of_app_iso_module`, private,
  upstream-PR candidate; mirrors the existing
  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` for
  the `SheafOfModules` variant in
  `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean:132`):
  ```lean
  private theorem isIso_of_app_iso_module {C : Type*} [Category C]
      {R : CßĄÆßĄ¢ Ōźż RingCat} {M N : PresheafOfModules R}
      (f : M Ō¤Č N) (h : ŌłĆ X, IsIso (f.app X)) : IsIso f := by
    rw [ŌåÉ isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R),
        NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
    intro X
    exact Functor.map_isIso (forgetŌéé (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat) (f.app X)
  ```
  **Critical caveat**: without
  `import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Functor.ReflectsIso.Balanced` (or a
  transitive import that pulls it in), the
  `(PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R).ReflectsIsomorphisms` typeclass
  does not synthesise. The chain
  `Balanced + Faithful ŌåÆ ReflectsIsomorphisms` is the load-bearing
  Mathlib infrastructure.
  **Use case**: any `PresheafOfModules`-level iso construction
  that defers the categorical work to per-open ModuleCat-level
  iso checks (e.g., chart-localised arguments using
  `KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv.symm` per affine open).

- **`simp` does NOT fire inside `ModuleCat.Derivation.mk` / `PresheafOfModules.Derivation'.mk` lambdas ŌĆö use `have / change / rw / exact`**
  *(NEW iter-138 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by the iter-138 PARTIAL
  prover lane on
  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two`
  at `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:547`; codified in
  `debug_feedback.md` and `task_results/Cotangent_GrpObj.lean.md` ┬¦
  "Negative lessons")*:
  When constructing a derivation via `ModuleCat.Derivation.mk
  (fun b => f.d (Žå b)) _ _ _` (or the presheaf variant
  `PresheafOfModules.Derivation'.mk`), the additive (`d_add`) and
  multiplicative (`d_mul`) proof obligations are stated with the inner
  function appearing as a **beta-redex** in the goal. `simp` with the
  obvious lemma names (`map_add`, `map_mul`, `ModuleCat.Derivation.d_add`,
  `ModuleCat.Derivation.d_mul`) does NOT fire because the lemma
  patterns expect `f (a + b)` or `f.d (a + b)` applied syntactically,
  but the goal carries `(fun b Ōå” f.d (Žå b)) (a + b)` and `simp` does
  not beta-reduce through the lambda before applying the simp set.
  **Working pattern** (validated iter-138 by closing the `d_add` /
  `d_mul` laws of `basechange_along_proj_two_inv_derivation`):
  ```lean
  fun a b => by
    have h : ((Žł.app X).hom (a + b)) =
             ((Žł.app X).hom a) + ((Žł.app X).hom b) :=
      RingHom.map_add _ _ _
    change (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D _).d _ = _
    rw [h]
    exact ModuleCat.Derivation.d_add _ _ _
  ```
  Four steps: (1) extract the additivity/multiplicativity identity
  for the inner function `Žå` into a `have h`; (2) `change` to reshape
  the outer goal so the `KaehlerDifferential.D` (or other derivation
  carrier) is exposed for rewriting; (3) `rw [h]` to apply the
  extracted identity; (4) `exact` the explicit derivation law. The
  same pattern works for any `ModuleCat.Derivation.mk` /
  `PresheafOfModules.Derivation'.mk` construction whose inner function
  is non-trivial (composed of multiple ring/algebra maps).
  **Generalisation**: for derivation constructions with multi-step
  inner functions, the `have / change / rw / exact` pattern is
  preferable to `simp` even when `simp` would close the goal in
  principle ŌĆö `simp`'s failure mode here is silent (it just doesn't
  fire) and is easy to mistake for a missing lemma. Codified.

- **`PresheafOfModules.pullback` is OPAQUE on `.obj`/`.map`; bypass via the adjunction transpose to the transparent `.pushforward`**
  *(NEW iter-137 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by the iter-137 PARTIAL
  prover lane on
  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two`
  at `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:500`)*:
  Mathlib defines `PresheafOfModules.pullback` as
  `(pushforward Žå).leftAdjoint` (file
  `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pullback:44`). The
  `leftAdjoint` definition is **opaque on `.obj`/`.map`** ŌĆö given
  `M : PresheafOfModules S` and an open `V : DßĄÆßĄ¢`, the chart-wise
  module `((PresheafOfModules.pullback Žå).obj M).obj V` does NOT
  reduce or unfold to a recognisable tensor-product form. This blocks
  every chart-by-chart route that needs to construct a derivation
  `D : RHS.Derivation' Žå'` or a section iso `app V : LHS.obj V Ōēģ RHS.obj V`
  on the RHS. **Two escape routes**:
  1. **Build a `pullbackObjEquivTensor` chart-unfolding helper**
     (~30ŌĆō60 LOC infrastructure): goes through
     `PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction`'s unit/counit
     to express the chart-wise sections as
     `TensorProduct ((F.op ŌŗÖ S).obj V) (R.obj V) (M.obj (F.op.obj V))`.
     Once landed, the full chart-by-chart recipe (Step 1 = chart-level
     `Algebra.IsPushout`-from-affine-product, Step 3 = chart-wise
     derivation `D_V`, Step 4 = `KaehlerDifferential.lift`, Step 5 =
     `PresheafOfModules.isoMk` assembly) becomes tractable.
  2. **Adjunction-transpose to the TRANSPARENT side**: a morphism
     `(pullback Žå).obj M ŌåÆ N` corresponds bijectively (via
     `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.homEquiv.symm`) to a morphism
     `M ŌåÆ (pushforward Žå).obj N`. The right-hand side
     `(pushforward Žå).obj N` IS transparent on `.obj`/`.map`
     (`pushforward = pushforwardŌéĆ ŌŗÖ restrictScalars`, with both
     pieces carrying `@[simps]` at
     `ModuleCat/Presheaf/Pushforward.lean:39, 86`), so a derivation /
     iso construction targeting this side proceeds without
     chart-unfolding. **Tradeoff**: this route only gives an
     `inverse` direction (RHS ŌåÆ LHS) directly; the forward direction
     (LHS ŌåÆ RHS) needs a separate step (e.g.,
     `isIso_of_isInverse` + a constructed forward morphism, OR
     `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_locally_surjective`-style local-iso
     argument).
  **Concrete skeleton (validated as compiling-typeable via
  `lean_run_code` iter-137)** for Route 2's inverse direction:
  ```lean
  noncomputable def basechange_inv ... := by
    let Žł : S Ō¤Č F.op ŌŗÖ R := <compatibility>
    let LHS := <left-hand presheaf-of-modules>
    let MG : PresheafOfModules S := <source>
    let D : ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward Žł).obj LHS).Derivation' Žå_MG := sorry
    let univ_step := (DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' Žå_MG).desc D
    let adj := PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction Žł
    exact (adj.homEquiv MG LHS).symm univ_step
  ```
  The body reduces to a single sub-goal: construct the derivation `D`
  on the transparent `(pushforward Žł).obj LHS` (~100ŌĆō200 LOC =
  pointwise pre-composition + Leibniz + `d_app` verifications).
  **Recommended use**: prefer Route 1 (chart-unfolding helper) when
  the helper is also useful as reusable infrastructure beyond the
  immediate consumer; prefer Route 2 when the immediate iter wants
  to ship under a tight sorry-count budget and the consumer doesn't
  need the bidirectional simp lemmas `isoMk` provides.

- **`change` over `show` for definitional-equality carrier reshapes
  through `PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp`**
  *(NEW iter-136 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced + codified by the iter-136
  prover lane closing
  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_restrict_along_identity_section`
  at `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:508`)*:
  When `PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp.app ╬®` merges two nested
  `PresheafOfModules.pullback` along scheme-morphism compatibility
  morphisms (built via `Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom`), the combined
  compatibility morphism it produces ŌĆö
  ```
  (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f.left).hom Ōē½
    (Opens.map f.left.base).op.whiskerLeft
      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom g.left).hom
  ```
  ŌĆö is **definitionally equal** to
  `(Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (g.left Ōē½ f.left)).hom`, by
  `Scheme.Hom.c` of a composition (`LocallyRingedSpace.comp_c`) +
  `whiskerRight` distributing over `whiskerLeft`. Lean's `change` tactic
  accepts this reshape without any rewriting; `show` does NOT (the Lean
  linter warns "Tactic 'show' has changed the goal" for genuine shape
  changes ŌĆö `show` is for surface-syntax aliasing only, `change` is for
  definitional reshapes). Recipe:
  ```lean
  refine iso1 Ōē¬Ōē½ ?_ Ōē¬Ōē½ iso2.symm   -- iso1, iso2 from pullbackComp.app
  refine eqToIso ?_
  change (PresheafOfModules.pullback (toRingCatSheafHom (lift_lhs)).hom).obj ╬® =
         (PresheafOfModules.pullback (toRingCatSheafHom (lift_rhs)).hom).obj ╬®
  rw [<scheme-morphism-equality-lemma>]                                -- closes
  ```
  Net cost: ~22 LOC body + ~5 LOC for the named scheme-morphism-equality
  helper. Reusable for any "pullback-of-pullback Ōēģ pullback-of-pullback"
  iso whose backbone is a categorical identity at the underlying scheme
  level.

- **`refine iso1 Ōē¬Ōē½ ?_ Ōē¬Ōē½ iso2.symm`** for iso chains with a hole
  *(NEW iter-136 Archon canonical ŌĆö same prover lane as above)*:
  The `Iso.trans` API does **not** accept the literal `sorry` token in
  chained position. Writing `iso1.trans (sorry.trans iso2.symm)` triggers
  `Unknown identifier '┬½sorry┬╗.trans'` (`sorry` is not a valid name in
  the dotted-method position, despite being a term elsewhere). The clean
  Mathlib alternative is the `Ōē¬Ōē½`-notation form combined with `refine`:
  ```lean
  refine iso1 Ōē¬Ōē½ ?_ Ōē¬Ōē½ iso2.symm
  ```
  produces a clean intermediate iso goal for the hole `?_`. Use this
  whenever you need a hole in the middle of an iso composition (the
  same pattern works with `Ōē½` for morphisms via
  `refine f Ōē½ ?_ Ōē½ g`).

- **Canonical-section identity in `Over (Spec k)` for a `GrpObj G`**
  *(NEW iter-136 Archon canonical ŌĆö `section_snd_eq_identity_struct`
  helper at `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:452`)*:
  The categorical identity `s Ōē½ pr_2 = ╬Ę_G Ōłś ŽĆ_G` in `Over (Spec k)`,
  where `s := lift (­Ø¤Ö G) (toUnit G Ōē½ ╬Ę[G])` is the canonical section of
  the first projection of the binary product, lifts to the underlying
  scheme level as
  `s.left Ōē½ pr_2.left = G.hom Ōē½ ╬Ę[G].left`.
  Closable in ~5 LOC via `Mathlib`'s rfl-level identities:
  ```lean
  private lemma section_snd_eq_identity_struct
      (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] :
      (lift (­Ø¤Ö G) (toUnit G Ōē½ ╬Ę[G])).left Ōē½ (snd G G).left =
        G.hom Ōē½ (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left ╬Ę[G]) := by
    rw [ŌåÉ Over.comp_left, lift_snd, Over.comp_left, Over.toUnit_left]
    rfl
  ```
  **The trailing `rfl` is load-bearing** ŌĆö it bridges the human-named
  `CommaMorphism.left ╬Ę[G]` (which appears in docstrings) with Lean's
  auto-elaborated `╬Ę.left`. These are equal but NOT syntactically
  identical to `rw`. Minimal binders (only `(G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
  [GrpObj G]`; no `n` / `SmoothOfRelativeDimension` / `IsProper` /
  `GeometricallyIrreducible` ŌĆö these are not needed for the categorical
  identity). The helper is the load-bearing close of any "Step 3"-shape
  argument that needs to identify the two ways of composing pullbacks
  implementing the section identity `pr_2 Ōłś s = ╬Ę_G Ōłś ŽĆ_G` at the
  presheaf-of-modules level (combined with the
  `change`-over-`pullbackComp` pattern above).

- **Binary-product shear-iso closure recipe**
  *(NEW iter-134 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced + codified by the iter-134
  prover lane on `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.shearMulRight` at
  `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:349`)*:
  When closing the inverse-pair `hom_inv_id` / `inv_hom_id` proofs of a
  binary-product shear iso `Žā : G ŌŖŚ G Ōēģ G ŌŖŚ G` in a Cartesian monoidal
  category `C` over `GrpObj G` data (with `Žā.hom := lift (fst G G) ╬╝`,
  `Žā.inv := lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G Ōē½ ╬╣) (snd G G) Ōē½ ╬╝)`), the
  Mathlib template `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight`'s bulk `simp_only`
  chain DOES NOT transfer ŌĆö the binary-product version has 2 free
  inputs (`fst`, `snd`) where `mulRight` has 1, so `comp_lift_assoc`
  matches too eagerly and the goal stays out of `lift_fst_snd`-reducible
  form. Recipe that works:
  ```lean
  apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
  ┬Ę simp                                                 -- fst coord
  ┬Ę rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, lift_snd]
    rw [ŌåÉ Category.assoc, comp_lift]
    rw [ŌåÉ Category.assoc, lift_fst, lift_snd]
    -- Goal: `lift (fst G G Ōē½ ╬╣) ╬╝ Ōē½ ╬╝ = snd G G`
    -- (or symmetric `lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G Ōē½ ╬╣) (snd G G) Ōē½ ╬╝) Ōē½ ╬╝ = snd G G`
    --  for inv_hom_id; positions of `╬╝` and `╬╣` flip)
    rw [show (lift (fst G G Ōē½ ╬╣) ╬╝ Ōē½ ╬╝ : G ŌŖŚ G Ō¤Č G) =
        lift (lift (fst G G Ōē½ ╬╣) (fst G G) Ōē½ ╬╝) (snd G G) Ōē½ ╬╝ from by
          rw [MonObj.lift_lift_assoc, lift_fst_snd, Category.id_comp],
      CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_left,           -- or `lift_comp_inv_right`
      MonObj.lift_comp_one_left]
  ```
  **Key insight**: structural rewrites (`Category.id_comp`, `lift_snd`,
  `comp_lift`, `lift_fst`) must precede the `lift_lift_assoc` rewrite,
  AND the inner `╬╝`-expansion to `lift (fst G G) (snd G G) Ōē½ ╬╝` must
  happen at exactly the right pattern position via a single `show`-
  equation (NOT `conv_lhs`, which matches both occurrences of `╬╝`).
  This NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL contribution candidate (Mathlib has no
  packaged binary-product shear iso) can be upstreamed once stable. ~30
  LOC per inverse-pair, kernel-only axioms.

- **The `Nonempty (X Ōēģ X)` tautological-placeholder anti-pattern (DO NOT REPEAT)**
  *(NEW iter-134 Archon canonical NEGATIVE finding ŌĆö surfaced by both
  `lean-auditor-review134` and
  `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-cotangent-grpobj-review134` as
  must-fix-this-iter on 3 load-bearing declarations in
  `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:476/508/566`)*:
  When the prover lane needs to land a named declaration for a
  multi-iter Mathlib-gap target (e.g., a deferred sheaf-level base-change
  iso), **DO NOT** ship the body as
  `theorem foo : Nonempty (X Ōēģ X) := Ō¤©Iso.refl _Ō¤®` with the intended
  substantive type recorded only in the docstring. This pattern:
  - Compiles cleanly with kernel-only axioms (because `Iso.refl _` is
    a real term, not `sorry`).
  - Emits **zero machine-readable incompleteness signal** ŌĆö
    `sorry_analyzer`, `lean_diagnostic_messages`, and `sync_leanok` all
    classify the declaration as complete; the deterministic
    `\leanok`-sync phase will add `\leanok` to the blueprint's proof
    block because the Lean compiles.
  - Triggers the strict rubric of `lean-auditor` + `lean-vs-blueprint-checker`
    as "weakened-wrong-definition + signature mismatch + excuse-comment"
    on the load-bearing declaration. `\notready` on the blueprint side
    + intended-type prose in the docstring **does not** clear the
    rubric.
  - Leaves the dependency surface broken: parameters like
    `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`, `[IsProper G.hom]`,
    `[GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]` declared on the placeholder are
    **unused** by `Iso.refl _`, a Lean-side smoke signal of the
    body/type mismatch (Lean's unused-variable hints fire under strict
    settings).
  **PREFERRED PATTERN** when a multi-iter Mathlib-gap target needs a
  scaffold: write the **intended** type in the signature and ship the
  body as `sorry`. Mirrors `Jacobian.lean:194-215`'s
  `positiveGenusWitness` honest scaffold (audited iter-134 by
  `lean-auditor-review134` and classified as honest). Example, taking
  `mulRight_globalises_cotangent` from iter-134:
  ```lean
  -- BAD (iter-134 anti-pattern):
  theorem mulRight_globalises_cotangent (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) ... :
      Nonempty (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom Ōēģ
                Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom) :=
    Ō¤©Iso.refl _Ō¤®

  -- GOOD (honest scaffold):
  noncomputable def mulRight_globalises_cotangent
      (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) ... :
      Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom Ōēģ
        (PresheafOfModules.pullback (Žå_str G)).obj
          ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (Žå_╬Ę G)).obj
            (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)) :=
    sorry
  ```
  The honest scaffold:
  - Emits a downstream-visible `sorry` (sorry counter increments).
  - Pins the intended signature for the iter-135+ prover lane to fill in.
  - Uses the declared instance parameters (because the intended type
    references them through `PresheafOfModules.pullback (Žå_str G)`).
  - Aligns with the project's iter-127-onward scaffold convention
    (`genusZeroWitness` iter-127, `nonempty_jacobianWitness` Phase-C,
    `rigidity_over_kbar` iter-126, `positiveGenusWitness` iter-134).
  **Codified rule**: NEVER ship `Nonempty (X Ōēģ X)` as a stand-in for a
  substantive iso. If the iter cannot produce the intended type
  signature (because the supporting infrastructure ŌĆö e.g. `Žå_*`
  compatibility morphisms ŌĆö does not yet exist), then either (a)
  produce the supporting infrastructure in the same iter, or (b) DEFER
  the declaration entirely until the iter that can produce it. Do not
  introduce a hollow placeholder declaration into the environment.

- **Honest sorry-bodied scaffold refactor: the canonical fix for a `Nonempty (X Ōēģ X) := Ō¤©Iso.refl _Ō¤®` placeholder pattern**
  *(NEW iter-135 Archon canonical POSITIVE finding ŌĆö the workflow
  that resolved 3 must-fix-this-iter findings from iter-134 review
  audits cleanly in 1 plan-only iter)*:
  When a prior iter has shipped a load-bearing target as
  `theorem foo : Nonempty (X Ōēģ X) := Ō¤©Iso.refl _Ō¤®` (the iter-134
  anti-pattern; see the related Knowledge Base entry above), the
  canonical iter-N+1 remediation is a refactor lane that:
  1. **Dispatches a `mathlib-analogist` first** to verify (via
     `lean_run_code` elaboration check) that the intended substantive
     RHS type elaborates cleanly against the current project state.
     This is decision (D) of a 4-part analogist verdict structure
     ((A) which Mathlib idiom to use; (B) literal Lean text for the
     intended types; (C) whether to introduce new project `def`s for
     the compatibility morphisms; (D) `lean_run_code` elaboration
     check). Produces a persistent file at `analogies/<slug>.md`.
  2. **Lands a refactor lane** swapping each placeholder
     `theorem ... : Nonempty (X Ōēģ X) := Ō¤©Iso.refl _Ō¤®` to a
     `noncomputable def ... : <intended-substantive-RHS> := sorry`.
     The signature is now what the math claims; the `sorry` body
     emits a downstream-visible incompleteness signal that
     `sorry_analyzer`, `lean_diagnostic_messages`, and `sync_leanok`
     can all see.
  3. **Has no prover dispatch in the refactor iter**
     (`planValidate.status: ok_intentional_skip`,
     `prover.durationSecs: 0`). The next iter dispatches the prover
     lane against the corrected signatures.
  4. **Codifies the convention** in a section docstring at the
     refactor's edit site naming the canonical compatibility-morphism
     idiom (in the iter-135 instance: `Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom`
     for `PresheafOfModules.pullback`, contrasted with the
     adjunction-transpose helper) so future iters cannot accidentally
     re-introduce the placeholder pattern.
  **Iter-135 instance**: closed 3 must-fix-this-iter findings (3
  weakened-wrong placeholders at `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:476/508/566`
  ŌåÆ 3 honest sorry-bodied scaffolds at L468/L496/L560) in 1
  plan-only refactor + writer iter. Sorry count 4 ŌåÆ 6 ŌĆö but BOTH
  review-phase audits (`lean-auditor-review135`,
  `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-cotangent-grpobj-review135`)
  independently classified the refactor as a "legitimate honesty
  improvement" (`lean-auditor` verbatim verdict). The 4 ŌåÆ 6 increase
  reflects 3 placeholder-pattern declarations becoming machine-
  readable as incomplete (which they always were ŌĆö `Iso.refl _`
  hides the incompleteness from `sorry_analyzer`). **Net semantic-
  health delta: materially positive.** **Documented at
  `iter/iter-135/plan.md`, `iter/iter-135/review.md`, and
  `analogies/phi-compatibility-morphisms.md`.**

- **Plan-phase deepening over low-quality prover dispatch when a HARD GATE fires**
  *(NEW iter-133 Archon canonical ŌĆö the workflow pattern that produced
  a clean iter-133 close despite no prover lane)*:
  When the iter's mandatory `blueprint-reviewer` returns a HARD GATE
  DEFER on a planned prover target (because the corresponding blueprint
  section is under-spec'd ŌĆö missing Lean signature stub, name-vs-
  construction ambiguity, missing named Mathlib lemma for a helper),
  the correct iter shape is **plan-phase deepening** (parallel
  blueprint-writer to harden the section + parallel mathlib-analogist
  if a design decision is needed + parallel refactor for any
  stale-framing cleanup the iter-N-1 review-phase audit flagged) and
  an `ok_intentional_skip` recorded in `meta.json`'s
  `planValidate.status`. The 1-iter latency cost of staging the prover
  lane is small compared to the cost of formalizing a broken blueprint
  the next iter throws away. Iter-133 instance: blueprint-reviewer's
  HARD GATE on `lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises` triggered a parallel
  blueprint-writer (chapter 324 ŌåÆ 511 LOC) + mathlib-analogist (PROCEED
  + ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB on sheaf-level RHS, persistent file at
  `analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md`) + refactor (5 docstring
  refreshes + 1 style nit on `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean`); iter-134
  prover lane is now staged with bills-of-materials decomposed into
  4 sub-pieces (~30ŌĆō60 + ~150ŌĆō300 + ~30ŌĆō80 + ~30ŌĆō60 LOC = 210ŌĆō440 LOC
  envelope). **Rule**: when a blueprint-reviewer's HARD GATE DEFER
  fires, do NOT silently dispatch the prover anyway "to make progress";
  the iter should pivot to plan-phase deepening + record the skip in
  PROGRESS.md's `## Current Objectives` with `(no prover dispatch this
  iter ŌĆö see iter/iter-NNN/plan.md for rationale)`. **Documented at
  `iter/iter-133/plan.md` and `iter/iter-133/review.md`.**

- **Sheaf-level RHS phrasing avoids chart-non-canonicity cocycle issues for `mulRight`-equivariance of cotangent constructions**
  *(NEW iter-133 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by
  `mathlib-analogist-mulright-globalises-iter133` Decision 4 +
  documented at `analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md`)*:
  When the project needs to express `mulRight`-equivariance of a
  cotangent-like construction on a smooth group scheme `G/k`, the
  iter-133 mathlib-analogist recommends phrasing the RHS at the
  **`PresheafOfModules` (sheaf) level** ŌĆö `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf
  G.hom Ōēģ (PresheafOfModules.pullback (Žå_str G)).obj ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (Žå_╬Ę G)).obj (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom))`
  ŌĆö rather than at the **value level** `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G`.
  Reason: the iter-131 chart-base-changed body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`
  depends on a chart `V` extracted via `Classical.choose` from the
  `smooth_locally_free_omega` existential. Under right-translation
  `mulRight_g`, the chart set `mulRight_g(V)` need not equal `V`,
  creating a chart-set cocycle concern at the value level. Phrasing
  the RHS at the sheaf-of-modules level puts the Žā-iso composition
  entirely at the presheaf-of-modules level where the chart non-
  canonicity does not enter, AND decouples the (chart-localisation
  identification) bridge to `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` into a separate
  ~100ŌĆō200 LOC lemma consumed in piece (i.c), NOT inside piece (i.b)'s
  body. The 4-step closure chain (shear iso ŌåÆ base-change of
  differentials ŌåÆ restriction along identity section ŌåÆ main
  composition) all lives at the presheaf level. Iter-133 instance:
  the recommendation pinned piece (i.b)'s envelope at 210ŌĆō440 LOC
  (NOT the 300ŌĆō800 LOC value-level variant). The companion side-
  effect: iter-130 `strategy-critic` Q2 worry about a deferred
  `(B)ŌåÆ(A)` bridge resurfacing is REFUTED ŌĆö the sheaf-level RHS
  bypasses it entirely. **Documented at
  `analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md` Decision 4 and
  `iter/iter-133/plan.md` ┬¦ "Response to critics".**

- **`Classical.choose`-chain parallel extraction for rank lemmas against opaque body shapes**
  *(NEW iter-132 Archon canonical ŌĆö the technique that closed
  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` in ~40 LOC after 4 iters of
  body-shape work)*:
  When the body of a `noncomputable def` is built from a chain of
  `Classical.choose` / `Classical.choose_spec` extractions on an
  existential `h : Ōłā X, P X`, a downstream rank-style lemma whose goal
  targets the body's underlying carrier (e.g. `Module.finrank k (someDef G) = n`)
  can be proven by **reconstructing the same chain locally inside the
  lemma's proof**. Because the same `Classical.choose` call (with the
  same arguments to `h`) produces the *same* term (Lean's definitional
  equality is reflexive on `Classical.choose`-applied-to-the-same-
  proof), the goal's `someDef G` and the proof's
  `(ModuleCat.extendScalars Žł).obj (ModuleCat.of ŌĆ” ╬®[ŌĆ”])` form share the
  same underlying carrier ŌĆö exposable via `change` to the explicit
  `TensorProduct ŌĆ”` form via `ModuleCat.ExtendScalars.obj'`'s
  definitional unfolding. Recipe:
  ```lean
  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom xŌéĆ
  set U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose with hU_def
  set V : G.left.Opens := h.choose_spec.choose with hV_def
  set e : V Ōēż G.hom Ōü╗┬╣ßĄü U := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose with he_def
  have hxV : xŌéĆ Ōłł V := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
  have hfree : Module.Free ŌĆ” := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.1
  have hrank : Module.rank ŌĆ” = Ōåæn := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.2
  -- (install algebra structures via letI, ŽłV via let, Nontrivial via haveI ŌĆ”)
  change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ŌĆ” k ╬®[ŌĆ”]) = n
  rw [Module.finrank_baseChange (R := k) (S := ŌĆ”) (M' := ╬®[ŌĆ”])]
  exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank
  ```
  This avoids the cost of building a `_eq_extendScalars`-style strong
  acceptance lemma + `obtain Ō¤©U, V, e, htop, heqŌ¤® := acceptance` +
  `rw [heq]` chain (which iter-131 also installs in
  `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:198` as a defensive
  alternative). **Key insight**: `Classical.choose` is opaque but
  *deterministic on equal proofs*; two `Classical.choose`-chains on
  the same existential reduce to definitionally-equal terms. This is
  precisely the structural property the iter-131 body refactor
  delivered (per iter-131 mathlib-analogist
  `analogies/cotangent-body-shape.md`); iter-132 is the closure
  test that proved the design works for downstream rank lemmas.

- **`RingHom.domain_nontrivial` discharges `Nontrivial` instance on the source of a ring map into a field**
  *(NEW iter-132 Archon canonical ŌĆö closed the `StrongRankCondition`
  instance hypothesis chain for `Module.finrank_baseChange`)*:
  When `Module.finrank_baseChange` (or similar Mathlib finrank-base-
  change lemmas) requires `[Nontrivial S]` on the source ring `S` of a
  base-change `S ŌåÆ R`, and the project context provides only a ring
  map `(someRingMap : S ŌåÆ+* R)` into a (nontrivial) field `R`, the
  one-liner discharge is:
  ```lean
  haveI : Nontrivial S := someRingMap.domain_nontrivial
  ```
  Combined with Mathlib's `commRing_strongRankCondition` instance
  (`Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.FreeModule.StrongRankCondition`), this
  completes the `StrongRankCondition S` hypothesis chain that
  `Module.finrank_baseChange` requires. Much cleaner than going
  through `IsAffineOpen V`-of-a-nonempty-affine reasoning. Iter-132
  instance: discharged `Nontrivial ╬ō(G.left, V)` via
  `ŽłV.hom.domain_nontrivial` where `ŽłV : ╬ō(G.left, V) Ō¤Č CommRingCat.of k`.

- **`change` vs `show` for definitional-equality carrier shifts**
  *(NEW iter-132 Archon canonical ŌĆö Lean linter warning surfaced during
  the iter-132 rank-lemma close, resolved by swapping to `change`)*:
  When a goal's carrier (e.g. `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G : ModuleCat k`)
  is *definitionally equal* but not syntactically equal to a target
  form (e.g. `TensorProduct ╬ō(G,V) k ╬®[ŌĆ”]`), use `change` rather than
  `show`. The Lean linter actively warns on `show` when it observes
  the tactic actually shifting the goal:
  > The `show` tactic should only be used to indicate intermediate
  > goal states for readability. However, this tactic invocation
  > changed the goal.
  `change` is the right primitive for genuine def-eq carrier shifts;
  `show` is reserved for verbatim restatement to aid readability.
  Both tactics share the same semantic effect (def-eq goal swap) but
  Lean treats them as structurally different. Iter-132 instance:
  `show Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ŌĆ”) = n` ŌåÆ warning;
  `change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ŌĆ”) = n` ŌåÆ silent. Both
  closed the proof; only `change` is linter-clean.

- **`Classical.choice (╬▒ := X) ?_` is the standard escape for "Type-valued `def` body depending on a `Prop`-level existential", but it discards content**
  *(NEW iter-130 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced as the critical iter-130
  finding flagged by `lean-auditor-review130` must-fix #1)*:
  When a `def` returning a `Type` (e.g. `ModuleCat k`) needs to use data
  from a `Prop`-level existential `Ōłā U V, P U V`, the elaborator
  forbids `obtain Ō¤©U, V, hŌ¤® := proof` directly with
  `recursor Exists.casesOn can only eliminate into Prop`. The standard
  workaround:
  ```lean
  refine Classical.choice (╬▒ := X) ?_
  obtain Ō¤©U, V, hŌ¤® := proof  -- now legal: Prop goal context Nonempty X
  exact Ō¤©theTermYouWant U V hŌ¤®
  ```
  **The result is NOT definitionally equal to `theTermYouWant U V h`** ŌĆö
  it is some unspecified inhabitant of `X` chosen by `Classical.choice`.
  Only `Nonempty X` is structurally recoverable; any downstream lemma
  about the structure of the inhabitant CANNOT be proved by unfolding
  the `def` and applying a fact about `theTermYouWant U V h`. **Use
  `Classical.indefiniteDescription` / chained `Classical.choose` /
  `Classical.choose_spec`** if any downstream proof needs to access
  the destructured data. Iter-130 instance:
  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` body wrapped the
  explicit chart-base-changed K├żhler module
  `(ModuleCat.extendScalars ŽłV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of ŌĆ” ╬®[ŌĆ”ŌüäŌĆ”])` in
  `Classical.choice (╬▒ := ModuleCat k)`. The body type-checks, passes
  `lean_verify` with kernel-only axioms, AND satisfies the
  progress-critic's vacuity-rule-out acceptance test (references
  `smooth_locally_free_omega`, 40 LOC, not simp-only) ŌĆö yet the
  iter-129+ rank lemma `finrank cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G = n` is
  **unprovable against this body** for the same structural reason
  iter-128's was. **Lesson**: in addition to "kernel-clean ŌēĀ
  mathematically correct" (iter-129 lesson #2), kernel-clean +
  acceptance-test-pass also do not guarantee structural-content-
  delivery to downstream consumers. A `Type`-valued `def` whose body
  uses an outer `Classical.choice` wrapper is opaque to definitional
  unfolding; rank/free/finite/dimension claims do not transport
  through it. **Documented at
  `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_130/summary.md` and ┬¦
  recommendations.md CRITICAL block.**

- **Lattice symbols (`ŌŖż`, `ŌŖź`, `ŌŖō`, `ŌŖö`) cannot be part of identifiers in tactic blocks**
  *(NEW iter-130 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced as a parse-error during
  the iter-130 body swap)*:
  Hypothesis names like `hŌŖż` parse partially in tactic blocks ŌĆö Lean
  tokenises the `ŌŖż` as the lattice top symbol and aborts the parse
  with `unexpected token 'ŌŖż'; expected ':=' or '|'`. Use ASCII-only
  identifiers (`htop`, `hbot`, `hsup`, `hinf`) for hypothesis names in
  tactic blocks. This is distinct from term-mode definitions (where
  `ŌŖż` survives, with surrounding whitespace), and from operator
  notations (where the symbol is a valid token). **Documented at
  `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_130/summary.md`.**

- **The unique-point collapse for `Spec` of a field: `(s : Ōåź(Spec (.of k)))` is always `default` via `Subsingleton.elim`**
  *(NEW iter-130 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced during the iter-130 body
  swap when establishing `(ŌŖż : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) Ōēż ╬Ęleft Ōü╗┬╣ßĄü V`)*:
  For `k` a field, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf`
  provides `Unique Ōåź(Spec (.of k))`, so any two terms of
  `Ōåź(Spec (.of k))` are equal via `Subsingleton.elim s default`. This
  turns any obligation of the form `s Ōłł U` for a point `s :
  Ōåź(Spec (.of k))` and an open `U ŌŖå Spec (.of k)` containing the
  unique point into a 1ŌĆō2 line proof:
  ```lean
  intro s _
  rw [show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
  exact <proof that default Ōłł U>
  ```
  Companion: `PrimeSpectrum.instUnique` in
  `Mathlib.RingTheory.Spectrum.Prime.Basic` gives the corresponding
  `Unique (PrimeSpectrum R)` instance for `[Field R]`. Reusable
  anywhere a proof needs to dispatch a `Spec k`-side point obligation.
  **Documented at `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_130/summary.md`.**

- **Presheaf-pullback at the top open of `Spec k` collapses to `k`; evaluating a presheaf-of-modules pullback along a `Spec k ŌåÆ X` morphism at `ŌŖż_X` discards the `X`-side structure**
  *(NEW iter-129 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced as a critical CORRECTION
  to iter-128 pattern #2; recorded by `mathlib-analogist-lieAlgebra-rank-bridge-iter129`)*:
  For `f : X Ō¤Č Spec k` (so `f.base : X.topology ŌåÆ Spec k`),
  `((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).obj (op ŌŖż)`
  is by definition the pointwise left Kan extension colimit over the
  indexing diagram `{V : Opens(Spec k) | f.base(ŌŖż_X) ŌŖå V}`. Since
  `Spec k` is single-point (only opens are `Ōłģ` and `ŌŖż`), the indexing
  diagram has a unique element `V = ŌŖż_{Spec k}`, and the colimit
  collapses to `╬ō(Spec k, ŌŖż) Ōēģ k` via `Scheme.╬ōSpecIso`. **Consequence
  for the iter-128 `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` construction**:
  `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom` evaluated at `op ŌŖż` is, via the
  `rfl` identification `relativeDifferentials'_obj`, the K├żhler module
  of `Žå'.app (op ŌŖż) : k ŌåÆ ╬ō(G, ŌŖż)`. For smooth proper geometrically
  irreducible `G/k`, smoothness ŌćÆ geometrically reduced ŌćÆ geometrically
  integral ŌćÆ `╬ō(G, ŌŖż) = k` (Stacks 0BUG; Mathlib's
  `AlgebraicGeometry.isField_of_universallyClosed`). So
  `Žå'.app (op ŌŖż) : k ŌåÆ k` is essentially the identity, and
  `KaehlerDifferential.subsingleton_of_surjective` gives `╬®[k/k] = 0`;
  the iter-128 body `(ModuleCat.extendScalars Žł.hom).obj 0 = 0` (the
  zero `k`-module) for the entire target class. **The iter-128 pattern
  #2** (`ModuleCat.extendScalars` of `presheaf.obj (op ŌŖż)` is the
  global-sections form of a presheaf-of-modules pullback) is
  **correct as an algebraic construction but mathematically degenerate**
  for the cotangent-at-identity application because the proper geom-irr
  hypothesis forces all global sections to land in `k`. Anyone needing
  a non-zero pullback-of-modules along a section over a proper
  geom-irr scheme must work either (a) on an affine chart `V Ōłŗ ╬Ę(pt)`
  via `smooth_locally_free_omega` (Replacement B in
  `analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`); (b) stalk-side via
  `Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace` (Replacement A; 500-1000 LOC);
  or (c) by sheafifying first (Replacement C; 800-2000 LOC). **Documented
  at `analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md` (full 5-step diagnostic +
  three-replacement comparison) and `proof-journal/sessions/session_129/summary.md`
  ┬¦ "Critical iter-129 discovery".**

- **Soundness lesson: kernel-clean does not imply mathematically correct**
  *(NEW iter-129 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced when the iter-128
  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` body, certified kernel-only by `lean_verify`
  with `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, was discovered to
  compute the zero `k`-module for the target class)*:
  `lean_verify <decl>` returning only kernel axioms certifies that the
  **proof** uses only kernel-allowed axioms (no `sorryAx`, no project
  axiom, no named-axiom escalation). It does **not** certify that the
  **statement** is mathematical content as intended. Definitions
  whose bodies type-check can encode the zero object, the empty type,
  or any other vacuous construction when the math claims something
  substantive. **Rule for future scaffolding rounds**: when a new
  declaration's Lean construction differs structurally from the
  blueprint's proof sketch (here: evaluate-then-extend-scalars on a
  *presheaf* vs the `­Øö¬/­Øö¬┬▓` *stalk* presentation the blueprint
  diagrams), dispatch a `mathlib-analogist` consult **before** locking
  the construction into a refactor ŌĆö even if `lake build` and
  `lean_verify` are both clean. The iter-128 review-phase
  `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-cotangent-grpobj-review128` flagged the
  bridge gap abstractly; the iter-129 analogist consult sharpened it
  to a concrete vacuity claim. **Lesson**: file-vs-blueprint bridges
  that exist in prose ("the bridge will be a future lemma") must be
  treated as load-bearing requirements that may not exist in Mathlib;
  if a `mathlib-analogist` cannot name the bridge, the construction
  needs revision. **Documented at `analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`
  Decision 1 + `proof-journal/sessions/session_129/summary.md` ┬¦ Key findings.**

- **`Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace` is the canonical Mathlib name** (not `IsRegularLocalRing.cotangentSpace`)
  *(NEW iter-129 Archon canonical ŌĆö minor; surfaced when the iter-129
  strategy-critic + blueprint-reviewer flagged the phantom name)*:
  The local-ring cotangent space `­Øö¬/­Øö¬┬▓` is `Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace R`
  in Mathlib `b80f227`, defined as `(maximalIdeal R).Cotangent` in
  `Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Cotangent`. The names
  `IsRegularLocalRing.cotangentSpace` and `LocalRing.cotangentSpace`
  do **not** exist ŌĆö `lean_local_search "cotangentSpace"` returns
  only `Algebra.Generators.cotangentSpaceBasis`,
  `Algebra.Extension.CotangentSpace`, and
  `Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace`. For the regular-local-ring
  finrank claim, the bridge is `IsRegularLocalRing` ŌåÆ
  `Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace` (not via `IsRegularLocalRing`'s
  own namespace). **Replace any future blueprint or strategy prose
  citing the phantom name.** Caught + corrected in iter-129's
  `RigidityKbar.tex` rewrite by `blueprint-writer-rigiditykbar-iter129`.

- **`Over.left` of the cartesian terminal object is definitionally the base; `CommaMorphism.left ╬Ę[G]` typechecks as a scheme morphism for free**
  *(NEW iter-128 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced during `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.lieAlgebra` body closure)*:
  In `Over (Spec (.of k))` with the cartesian monoidal structure, the
  unit object `(­Ø¤Ö_ (Over (Spec (.of k))))` has `.left = Spec (.of k)`
  by definition (via the `Over` terminal-object construction). This
  means that for any `[CategoryTheory.GrpObj G]` over `Spec k`, the
  scheme-level identity section is recoverable by ONE LINE:
  ```lean
  let ╬Ęleft : Spec (.of k) Ō¤Č G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left ╬Ę[G]
  ```
  No explicit coercion, no `Iso.hom`, no `letI`-typeclass jiggling.
  Reusable across the project for any `Over (Spec R)` `MonObj` /
  `GrpObj` whose scheme-level structure needs extracting. The `╬Ę[G]`
  notation is the `MonObj.one G : ­Ø¤Ö_ Ō¤Č G` notation from
  `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Mon_`. **Documented at
  `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_128/summary.md` ┬¦ Per-target
  detail and ┬¦ Key findings.**

- **`ModuleCat.extendScalars` of `presheaf.obj (op ŌŖż)` is the global-sections form of a presheaf-of-modules pullback along a scheme section**
  *(NEW iter-128 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced during `lieAlgebra` body closure as the cheaper alternative to `PresheafOfModules.pullback`)*:
  When the project needs to pull back a `PresheafOfModules` `M` on a
  scheme `X` along a section `╬Ę : Spec k Ō¤Č X` and only the global
  sections are needed, the cheap form is:
  ```lean
  let Žł : ╬ō(X, ŌŖż) Ō¤Č CommRingCat.of k :=
    ╬Ę.appTop Ōē½ (Scheme.╬ōSpecIso (.of k)).hom
  (ModuleCat.extendScalars Žł.hom).obj (M.obj (op ŌŖż))
  ```
  This evaluates the presheaf at the top open (a
  `ModuleCat (╬ō(X, ŌŖż))`), then extends scalars along `Žł` (tensoring
  with `k` over `╬ō(X, ŌŖż)`) to land in `ModuleCat k`. **Crucially,
  this bypasses the full `PresheafOfModules.pullback` functor**,
  whose `(pushforward Žå).IsRightAdjoint` instance is non-trivial in
  the scheme setting. Reusable any time the project needs a presheaf
  of modules' value at a section's image at the base level. Caveat:
  this is the global-sections form, NOT the canonical
  `colim`-over-open-neighborhoods pullback; the two agree for
  proper geometrically irreducible schemes (where `╬ō(X, O_X) = k`)
  but disagree on general presheaves. **Documented at
  `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_128/summary.md` ┬¦ Per-target
  detail and ┬¦ Key findings.**

- **Soundness lesson: "smallest signature surface" framing in a refactor directive can produce over-specialised signatures the prover then locks in**
  *(NEW iter-128 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced post-prover via
  `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-cotangent-grpobj-review128` MUST-FIX
  S-1 on `lieAlgebra`'s `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 G.hom]`
  hardcoding)*:
  When a refactor lane scaffolds a new declaration whose blueprint
  prose is dimension/property-agnostic (e.g. "smooth group scheme"
  with NO dimension constraint), the directive must pin a CONCRETE
  Lean signature stub (not just prose). The iter-128 directive said
  piece (i.a) has "smallest signature surface" but left dimension
  unspecified; the prover landed `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 G.hom]`,
  which is over-specialised relative to the blueprint AND makes the
  declaration unusable for the downstream consumer
  (`rigidity_over_kbar` applied to an abelian variety of arbitrary
  relative dimension). **Rule for future scaffolding refactors**:
  the directive's "reference body" code block MUST exactly specify
  the desired signature (e.g. `{n : ŌäĢ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n
  G.hom]` to share the dimension parameter with the iter-129+ rank
  lemma, or `[Smooth G.hom]` for the most-general form). Encoding
  notes that grant "prover discretion" should be paired with a
  concrete stub example, not just prose. **Documented at
  `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_128/recommendations.md`
  CRITICAL #1 and ┬¦ Strategic.**

- **`AlgEquiv.ofBijective` + `IsLocalization.lift_{injective,surjective}_iff` is the canonical narrowing step for "construct an AlgEquiv from an `IsLocalization.lift`"**
  *(NEW iter-124 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced during M1.b `commutes'`-closure + bijectivity narrowing on `appLE_isLocalization`)*:
  When the forward map is already an `IsLocalization.lift hg` to a
  target `B` whose algebra structure is `g.toAlgebra`, the
  cleanest way to produce an `AlgEquiv` is to promote `forward` to
  an explicit `AlgHom` (`forwardAlg`) and reduce the residual to
  `Function.Bijective ŌćæforwardAlg` via `AlgEquiv.ofBijective`. The
  bijectivity then decomposes via
  `IsLocalization.lift_injective_iff` (gives a precise injectivity
  criterion in terms of `algebraMap` equality vs `g` equality) and
  `IsLocalization.lift_surjective_iff` (gives a precise surjectivity
  criterion in terms of `v * g x.2 = g x.1` representability). This
  avoids the alternative route via `RingEquiv.ofRingHom backward +
  AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv` that requires constructing the backward
  map directly and verifying two inverse identities. Iter-124
  instance: `Differentials.lean:345-398` reduces the iter-123
  `Localization M ŌēāŌéÉ[╬ō(S, U)] A_colim` AlgEquiv residual to
  `Function.Bijective ŌćæforwardAlg` in 1 substantive Edit, keeping
  the per-file sorry count at exactly 1 and packaging Steps 2 + 3
  of the M1.b chain into a single named bijectivity claim. **Pairs
  naturally with the next pattern**: `commutes'` closes in 1 LOC
  via `RingHom.congr_fun`.
  ```lean
  let forwardAlg : Localization M ŌåÆŌéÉ[A] B :=
    { forward with
      commutes' := by
        intro r
        have h := RingHom.congr_fun h_fwd_comp r
        exact h }
  exact AlgEquiv.ofBijective forwardAlg ?_
  -- residual: Function.Bijective ŌćæforwardAlg
  ```
  **Documented at `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_124/summary.md`
  ┬¦ Per-target detail and ┬¦ Key findings**.

- **`RingHom.congr_fun h_fwd_comp r` closes the `commutes'` field of an AlgHom when target algebra is `someRingHom.toAlgebra`**
  *(NEW iter-124 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced during the iter-124 `forwardAlg` promotion)*:
  When (i) the target algebra structure is constructed from a ring
  map via `letI : Algebra A B := ringHom.toAlgebra` (or a top-level
  `@[reducible] noncomputable def`), and (ii) you have a fact
  `f.comp (algebraMap A C) = ringHom`, then the `commutes'` field
  of `{ f with commutes' := ... } : ... ŌåÆŌéÉ[A] B` closes in 1 LOC:
  ```lean
  commutes' := by
    intro r
    have h := RingHom.congr_fun h_fwd_comp r
    exact h
  ```
  The `RingHom.congr_fun` produces `f (algebraMap r) = ringHom r`;
  the goal needs `f (algebraMap r) = algebraMap r` on the RHS; the
  two RHS forms are defeq via the unfolding of
  `RingHom.toAlgebra`'s `algebraMap`, so `exact h` succeeds without
  any `show`/`change` rewrite. Companion to the iter-122 helper-
  extraction pattern (lifting `appLE_colimAlgebra` to a top-level
  `@[reducible] noncomputable def` makes the defeq available
  wherever needed). Iter-124 instance: `Differentials.lean:349-354`.
  **Documented at `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_124/summary.md`
  ┬¦ Key findings**.

- **`suffices` reduction to consolidate multi-step inverse-construction PARTIALs into a single residual sorry**
  *(NEW iter-123 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced during M1.b Step 1 + Step 4 closure on `appLE_isLocalization`)*:
  When a multi-step proof has steps 1 + N immediately constructible
  and intermediate steps jointly required to build a single object
  (an iso, an `AlgEquiv`, a `LinearEquiv`), use `suffices` to reduce
  to that object. This keeps the file's sorry count at exactly 1
  (vs. splitting the residual into separate sorries for each
  remaining step), AND makes the iter-N+1 prover's target explicitly
  the intermediate object. Companion to iter-119's "structurally-
  minimal residual-sorry cascade" (`constructor / all_goals first | exact _ | sorry`).
  ```lean
  -- Step 1: build the forward direction (concrete code).
  have forward_data : ... := ...
  -- Step N: reduce to the existential via a Mathlib constructor.
  suffices intermediate : SomeIsoType from
    Mathlib.someClosingLemma forward_data intermediate
  -- Steps 2 ŌĆ” N-1: the residual (single sorry here).
  sorry
  ```
  Iter-123 instance: `Differentials.lean:328ŌĆō331` reduces
  `IsLocalization M A_colim` via
  `IsLocalization.isLocalization_of_algEquiv M AE`
  to the single residual `AlgEquiv` `Localization M ŌēāŌéÉ[╬ō(S, U)] A_colim`.
  Companion: `IsLocalization.lift_comp` captures the compatibility
  witness `(IsLocalization.lift hg).comp algebraMap = g` for free
  ŌĆö use it to package the forward-map-composed-with-algebra-map
  fact for downstream Step-3 inverse-identity verification and
  Step-4 algebra-map compatibility. **Documented at
  `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_123/summary.md` ┬¦ Key findings**.

- **`IsLocalization.isLocalization_of_algEquiv` (not `_of_le` or `_of_ringEquiv`) is the canonical Step-4 closer for "colim of localizations" ŌåÆ `IsLocalization`**
  *(NEW iter-123 Archon canonical ŌĆö verified by iter-123 M1.b tactical mathlib-analogist)*:
  When proving `IsLocalization M B` via a constructed AlgEquiv
  `Localization M ŌēāŌéÉ[A] B` (the cleanest output of the two-direction
  forward/backward construction documented in the iter-121 Knowledge
  Base entry), the right Mathlib closer is
  `IsLocalization.isLocalization_of_algEquiv M : (Localization M ŌēāŌéÉ[A] B) ŌåÆ IsLocalization M B`
  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Basic:304`).
  The iter-122 plan-phase recommendation listed `IsLocalization.of_le` ŌĆö
  that lemma is for the *different* shape "extend an existing `IsLocalization
  M S` to `IsLocalization N S` for `M Ōēż N`" ŌĆö and is NOT directly applicable
  when only an iso is in hand. The "`isLocalization_iff_of_ringEquiv`"
  alternative is iff-shaped (less direct). The constructor requires an
  `AlgEquiv` (not a `RingEquiv`); package via `AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv` from
  a `RingEquiv` + algebra-map compatibility (the iter-123
  `Differentials.lean:348ŌĆō361` recipe sketch documents the assembly).
  **Documented at `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_123/summary.md` ┬¦ Key findings**.

- **`rw [Functor.map_comp]` fails on `Lan`-defined functors; pre-prove `hmc` with `Functor.map_comp _ _ _` and use `erw [hmc]`**
  *(NEW iter-122 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced during M1.b Step 0 closure)*:
  Goals shaped
  `((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).map (gŌéü Ōē½ gŌéé) = ...`
  cannot be advanced by `rw [Functor.map_comp]` (the rewriter's
  pattern match fails despite the printed-pattern-vs-target equality
  being literal). Diagnosis: the `Lan`-defined functor instance
  carries category-instance metadata that the rewriter's unification
  doesn't see through. **Workaround**: pre-prove the rewrite as a
  `have`, then use `erw` instead of `rw`:
  ```
  have hmc : Pf.map (gŌéü Ōē½ gŌéé) = Pf.map gŌéü Ōē½ Pf.map gŌéé :=
    Functor.map_comp _ _ _
  erw [hmc]
  ```
  Companion lessons:
  - `rw [Category.assoc]` similarly fails on the same goals;
    use `exact Category.assoc _ _ _` directly when the goal
    is exactly the associativity equation.
  - `adj.unit.naturality` produces an equation involving the identity
    functor `((­Ø¤Ł _).obj _)`; use `simpa using
    ((adj.unit.app S.presheaf)).naturality _` to clean it up.
  **Documented at `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_122/summary.md`
  ┬¦ Key findings.**

- **`change`/`show` on `algebraMap g` fails even when the algebra is `Žå.hom.toAlgebra`; route via `IsUnit` + `exact` instead**
  *(NEW iter-122 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced during M1.b Step 0 closure)*:
  When `letI : Algebra A B := Žå.hom.toAlgebra` is in scope and the
  goal involves `algebraMap A B g`, the `change` and `show` tactics
  fail to unify the goal with a form involving `Žå.hom g` directly,
  even though they ARE definitionally equal (`'change' tactic
  failed, pattern ... is not definitionally equal to target`).
  **Workaround**: route the conclusion via the equation form
  ```
  have h_alg_unfold : (algebraMap A B) g = Žå.hom g := rfl
  rw [h_alg_unfold]
  ```
  or via `RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra` when applicable. The `rfl`
  succeeds at the `have`-level even though `change` fails at the
  goal-level. For `IsUnit (algebraMap A B g)`-shaped goals,
  alternatively route through `IsUnit (Žå.hom g)` first via a
  helper `have h_factor`, then `exact` the result with the
  defeq absorbed. Reusable across the project.
  **Documented at `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_122/summary.md`
  ┬¦ Key findings.**

- **Helper-lemma extraction pattern: name top-level decls instead of inlining `letI`-stubs to make consumers re-usable**
  *(NEW iter-122 Archon canonical ŌĆö pattern paid off in M1 closure)*:
  When the plan-phase refactor introduces a sorry-bearing declaration
  whose body contains a `letI : Algebra A B := sorry` or similar
  typeclass instance, the temptation is to close the `letI` inline
  in the same iter. The better pattern is to lift the algebra
  structure out into a top-level `noncomputable def` (e.g.
  `appLE_colimAlgebra := (appLE_colimRingHom f e).hom.toAlgebra`),
  which makes the algebra re-usable across every consumer's scope.
  Companion: when the proof itself decomposes into "step 0 sub-claim
  + steps 1ŌĆōN main", extract step 0 as a named helper theorem
  (e.g. `isUnit_appLE_unitSubmonoid_in_colim`) before the main proof
  body; the named helper is then directly invokable as a hypothesis
  to `IsLocalization.lift` (Step 1) and similar consumers in the
  same iter or downstream iters. The iter-122 M1 closure used this
  pattern with `appLE_colimRingHom`, `appLE_colimAlgebra`,
  `appLE_colimRingHom_comp_ŽåV`, `isUnit_appLE_unitSubmonoid_in_colim`
  ŌĆö making the bridge body `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_equiv_kaehler_appLE`
  close in a single Edit after the helpers landed. Reusable across
  the project (cf. the iter-098 split-slot pattern with
  `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`).

- **`Functor.Final` is the WRONG framing for "directed colimit of localizations = localization at the union submonoid"; use `IsLocalization.of_le` with cocone universality**
  *(NEW iter-121 Archon canonical ŌĆö surfaced by `mathlib-analogist-bridge-iter121`)*:
  the natural-looking colim-comparison shape
  ```
  colim_{g Ōłł M, in the basic-open-cocone} A_g  Ōēā  Localization M A
  ```
  has no off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma in `b80f227` (verified via
  `lean_leansearch`, `lean_loogle`, `lean_local_search` on multiple
  variants). Trying to use `CategoryTheory.Functor.Final` to reduce
  to a cofinality-of-indexing-category argument leads into Mathlib
  territory that doesn't exist. The cleanly Mathlib-aligned path is
  to **bypass the colim-comparison entirely** and prove
  `IsLocalization M A_colim` directly:
  (a) Construct `A_M ŌåÆ A_colim` via `Localization M`'s universal
      property (after showing each `g Ōłł M` is a unit in `A_colim`).
  (b) Construct `A_colim ŌåÆ A_M` via the colim cocone's universal
      property, picking basic-open refinements `D(g) ŌŖå W` on each
      open and mapping `╬ō(S, W) ŌåÆ ╬ō(S, D(g)) = A_g ŌåÆ A_M`.
  (c) Verify the composites are identity via
      `IsLocalization.ringHom_ext`.
  (d) Conclude `IsLocalization M A_colim` via `IsLocalization.of_le`
      (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Defs`:

- **`by_cases h : genus C = 0` is a well-formed Lean decomposition for `nonempty_jacobianWitness`-style protected signatures with no genus parameter**
  *(NEW iter-121 Archon canonical ŌĆö strategy-critic-iter121 alternative #1 adopted)*:
  when a protected signature quantifies over a `Nat`-genus-implicit
  object (here: `C : Over (Spec (.of k))` with smoothness +
  irreducibility + properness instances but no `g : ŌäĢ` parameter),
  the body may still be a `Nat`-valued case split because
  `AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : ŌäĢ` is `DecidableEq`. The shape
  ```
  theorem nonempty_jacobianWitness ... := by
    by_cases h : AlgebraicGeometry.genus (k := k) C.left = 0
    ┬Ę exact Ō¤©genusZeroWitness C hŌ¤®      -- closed by M2
    ┬Ę exact Ō¤©positiveGenusWitness C (Nat.pos_of_ne_zero h)Ō¤®  -- closed by M3
  ```
  converts a "genus-stratified" prose proof into an explicit
  Lean-level decomposition, which the autonomous loop can then close
  one arm at a time. Reusable for any all-quantified protected
  signature whose informal proof case-splits on a decidable scalar.
  **Documented at `STRATEGY.md ┬¦ Decomposition: genus-stratified
  body of nonempty_jacobianWitness` (iter-121).**

- **Genus-0 over non-algebraically-closed `k` is NOT `C Ōēģ ŌäÖ┬╣_k`; use base change to `k╠ä` + Galois descent**
  *(NEW iter-121 Archon canonical ŌĆö strategy-critic-iter121 alternative #2 adopted)*:
  the iter-121 first-draft strategy claimed M2.c was the
  identification `C Ōēģ ŌäÖ┬╣_k` for genus-0 curves. This is
  **mathematically false** on the protected
  `nonempty_jacobianWitness` signature, which permits curves
  without `k`-rational points: BrauerŌĆōSeveri conics over `ŌäÜ`
  (`x┬▓ + y┬▓ + z┬▓ = 0` is a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
  curve of genus 0 with no `ŌäÜ`-point and is NOT isomorphic to
  `ŌäÖ┬╣_ŌäÜ`). The corrected route is: base change to `k╠ä` (where
  `C_{k╠ä}` is automatically nonempty geometrically integral, hence
  has a `k╠ä`-point and IS isomorphic to `ŌäÖ┬╣_{k╠ä}`); apply rigidity
  for `ŌäÖ┬╣_{k╠ä} ŌåÆ A_{k╠ä}`; Galois-descend constancy back to `k`.
  Reusable for any "X Ōēģ ŌäÖ┬╣_k under genus-0 + smooth + proper +
  geometrically irreducible" claim ŌĆö the `k`-rational-point
  hypothesis is silently required and is NOT implied by the named
  hypotheses. **Documented at `STRATEGY.md ┬¦ Roadmap M2` and
  `Jacobian.tex ┬¦ (C.2)` 7-sub-step expansion (iter-121).**

- **Presheaf-of-modules section at a single open is NOT defeq to the chart-local Mathlib ring-theoretic output ŌĆö unfold before claiming `rfl`**
  *(NEW iter-119 Archon canonical)*: a blueprint claim that
  `(relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj (.op VŌéĆ)` is
  "definitionally equal" to `╬®[╬ō(X,VŌéĆ)Ōüä╬ō(S,UŌéĆ)]` via the project-local
  `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler` (whose body is `rfl`) is
  **WRONG**. The `rfl` body identifies the section module with
  `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (Žå'.app (op VŌéĆ))` where the source
  ring of `Žå' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
  CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c` is
  `((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).obj
  (.op VŌéĆ)` ŌĆö i.e. the inverse-image presheaf at `VŌéĆ`, which by the
  left Kan extension formula computes to
  `colim_{f VŌéĆ ŌŖå W ŌŖå S} S.presheaf.obj W`. This is a colimit ring
  strictly larger than `╬ō(S, UŌéĆ)` in general; the two K├żhler
  differential modules are not definitionally the same type, and the
  bridge requires a real `LinearEquiv` constructed via cofinality of
  `UŌéĆ` in the colimit cone of neighbourhoods of `f VŌéĆ` conditional on
  `IsAffineOpen UŌéĆ Ōł¦ IsAffineOpen VŌéĆ Ōł¦ VŌéĆ Ōēż fŌü╗┬╣ßĄü UŌéĆ`. **Pattern**: when
  a blueprint claims "definitional equality" between a presheaf-of-
  modules construction and a ring-theoretic Mathlib output, *unfold the
  construction at the Lean level before accepting the claim*. The left
  Kan extension at a single open is generically not `rfl`-equal to the
  ring-of-sections-on-a-containing-open. **Documented at
  `Differentials.lean:130-134` (iter-119 prover Step-5 comment block)
  and `Differentials.tex:91-104` (`% NOTE (iter-119 prover finding)`

- **Structurally-minimal residual-sorry cascade: `constructor / all_goals first | <exact_a> | <exact_b> | sorry`**
  *(NEW iter-119 Archon canonical)*: when the body of a multi-conjunct

- **Bar-B scaffolding for single-sorry main theorems through multi-step recipes**
  *(NEW iter-112 Archon canonical)*: when a single-sorry "main theorem" needs
  to enact a multi-step recipe but the load-bearing content is one
  consolidated sub-claim, the right structural-advance shape is:
    1. **One load-bearing helper** with the load-bearing sub-claim, whose
       conclusion already uses the *characterisation namespace* that the
       multi-step recipe lands in (e.g. `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheafOpensLeCover`
       after Step 1 forgetful reduction, not `Presheaf.IsSheaf`); body
       `sorry`.
    2. **One derived helper**, fully closed, bridging from the load-bearing
       helper back to the recipe's intermediate goal-shape via the
       characterisation lemma (e.g. `isSheaf_iff_isSheafOpensLeCover.mpr`).
    3. **Main theorem body fully closed**: performs the leading reduction
       (e.g. `rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp _ _ (forget _)]`) and
       delegates to the derived helper.
  This shape keeps the file's sorry count flat at the current
  (project-tracked) value while exposing the single residual mathematical
  content as one named helper. Avoids two regressive anti-patterns:
  (v1) two identical-signature helpers (boilerplate); (v2) two

### User policy directives (iter-116 response in `USER_HINTS.md`)

After the iter-116 user-escalation pause, the user responded with five
policy-level rules that supersede any prior loop-internal framing:

1. **Self-decide best strategy** ŌĆö the user does not pick among
   planner-surfaced options. The loop must autonomously decide and
   commit, then explain the reasoning in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`.
2. **Strict correctness ŌĆö no temporarily-wrong statements** ŌĆö any wrong
   mathematical statement (definition, signature, proof) must be removed,
   and the gap filled (either with a correct closure or an honest
   disclosure that is itself mathematically clean). "Temporarily wrong"
   is no longer an acceptable state.
3. **Blueprints must be detailed enough for provers** ŌĆö chapters need
   enough mathematical material that a prover can formalize without
   re-deriving the content.
4. **`STRATEGY.md` must be cleanly organized ŌĆö strategy-only, no history**
   ŌĆö cumulative iter-by-iter achievement enumeration is policy-banned in
   STRATEGY.md. History belongs in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` sidecars and
   here in PROJECT_STATUS.md's iteration log.
5. **Nothing should be deferred** ŌĆö every active sorry needs a concrete
   closure plan with a budget. "Permanent named Mathlib gap" framing is
   no longer policy-acceptable. Named gaps remain valid as scaffolding,
   but each must have an active multi-iter closure route.

**These rules apply to the entire iter-117+ planning surface**, not only
the L175 route. Plan agents must verify every dispatched lane and every
STRATEGY.md edit against rules (2) and (5) before committing.

## Knowledge Base ŌĆö Proof patterns (iter-241 addition)

- **Stuck `restrictScalars`-iso naturality square ŌåÆ delete the `eqToIso`, then `ext x; rfl` (iter-241, `FlatBaseChange.lean`, the keystone fix).** When a chain of `restrictScalars`-based isos has a naturality square that resists ALL rewrite forms (`rw`/`simp`/`slice`/`conv`/`reassoc_of%`, ~15 tried over iters 237ŌĆō240, all failing to unify a `restrictScalarsComp'App ŌĆ” .inv` subterm despite identical printing): the blocker is a single `eqToIso`/`eqToHom` cast breaking definitional equality. **Recipe:** replace the `eqToIso (congrArg (fun h => (restrictScalars h).obj M) hcomp)` with the identity-on-element NatIso component `(ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr hcomp).app M` (built from `AddEquiv.refl`, clean `rfl` apply-lemma, SAME type, consumer-safe). All `restrictScalars*` repackaging maps (`restrictScalarsComp'App_{hom,inv}_apply`, `restrictScalarsCongr` app, `restrictScalars.map_apply`) are `rfl`-identity on the underlying carrier (`restrictScalars` never changes the carrier type); once the one `eqToHom` is gone, both legs are defeq and `ext x; rfl` closes the square with no rewrite-matching at all. - **`@asIso ŌĆ” (explicit witness)` to bundle an `Iso` when `asIso`/`infer_instance` fails at reducible transparency (iter-241, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** `pullbackObjUnitToUnit Žå` carries buried implicit instance args (`[F.IsContinuous]`, `[(pushforward Žå).IsRightAdjoint]`) that are defeq-but-not-syntactic when several `(Opens.map _).Final` are in scope, so `asIso (pbu g)` / `Functor.map_isIso _ _ (pbu g)` FAIL to synthesise `IsIso (pbu g)` (reducible transparency) even with a type-ascribed `haveI` in scope. **Fix (the `Functor.Monoidal.╬╝Iso` bundled-iso idiom):** (1) `private lemma isIso_pbu_of_final g [(Opens.map g.base).Final] : IsIso (pbu g) := inferInstance` isolates the lone `Final` so synthesis runs at a clean site; (2) `def pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso g := @asIso _ _ _ _ (pbu g) (isIso_pbu_of_final g)` passes the witness EXPLICITLY ŌĆö the `@asIso` typecheck is `isDefEq` at DEFAULT transparency, which succeeds where reducible synthesis does not. Asymmetry note: `have t : IsIso (pbu g) := inferInstance` (type-ascribed) WORKS because the expected type pins the implicits before synthesis; `asIso (pbu g)` does not. Always pass instances explicitly through `@asIso`.

- **`conjugateEquiv_whiskerRight` vs `whiskerLeft` discriminant.** For a goal factor of the form
  `(pullback h).map (g.app P) = (whiskerRight g (pullback h)).app P`, the correct mate device is
  `CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv_whiskerRight` (Mathlib `Mates.lean:536`), NOT `conjugateEquiv_whiskerLeft`
  (which gives `g.app ((pullback h).obj P)`). Namespace is bare `CategoryTheory.ŌĆ”`, not `ŌĆ”Adjunction.ŌĆ”`.
